revision.text:
{{神社| 名称= 細石神社 | 所在地=福岡県糸島市三雲432| 祭神=磐長姫命
木之花開耶姫命| 社格=村社| 例祭=10月25日 例大祭}} '''細石神社'''（さざれいしじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[糸島市]]にある神社。古くは''佐々禮石神社''と表記されていた。祭神は[[イワナガヒメ|磐長姫]]と[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花開耶姫]]の姉妹二柱。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は村社。 == 概要 == [[伊都国]]の中心部に所在すると推察されている。 [[元禄]]8年（[[1695年]]）の「細石神社御縁起」では、古くは神田も多く大社であった。しかし、たびたび兵乱に見舞われ社殿を焼失した。さらに[[天正]]15年（[[1587年]]）の[[豊臣秀吉]]の[[太閤検地]]により、神田没収に遭い衰退した。現在は「村社、細石神社」の石柱が鳥居の脇に建てられている。 == 考察 == '''細石神社'''は社殿の背面にある「[[三雲南小路遺跡]]（[[文政]]5年（[[1822年]]）発見）の拝殿」であろうと地元民は云っている。 この「細石神社」の祭神は「[[磐長姫]]、[[木花開耶姫]]」である。つまり主祭神は「女神二柱」である。この事からと、昔は「[[高祖神社]]から神輿が細石神社に下る(上る?)」神幸があったという事から、この「細石神社」は「三代実録」に記載された「[[元慶]]元年（[[877年]]）、'''高磯ヒメ神'''に従五位下を授ける」とある「高磯ヒメ神」であると考えられると'''三雲の氏子'''は云っている。 ちなみに通説で云う「[[高祖神社]]の祭神が高磯ヒメ神であろう」というのは「[[高祖神社]]の主祭神、[[ホオリ|彦火火出見尊]]」を侮辱する事になることをここに記す。 この「細石神社」は「[[三雲南小路遺跡]]の、[[ニニギ|日子番ノニニギ命]]と木之花咲耶姫の墓の拝殿」であると考えられる。 地元考古学者[[原田大六]]は、[[三雲南小路遺跡]]発掘調査の時に「1号甕棺墓は武器類が多く、2号甕棺墓は玉類が多い。そして、この二つの甕棺は寄り添うようにして埋葬されている事から、これは'''男と女の墓'''である。つまり、'''王と妃の墓'''である」とコメントしている。 ==交通== [[九州旅客鉄道|JR]][[筑肥線]][[波多江駅]]から井原山行バス約10分。三雲宮(細石神社)前下車。 == 参考文献 == *『怡土志摩地理全誌』由比章祐/著 *『鎮守の社』糸島神職会 ==外部リンク== *[http://blog.goo.ne.jp/noyamany/e/e9d92be44b6b385a34c4830c7616596b 細石神社（福岡県前原市） - 九州神社紀行−ブログ（個人サイト） ] {{DEFAULTSORT: さされいししんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:糸島市の神社]] [[Category:糸島市]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{Sakujo/本体|2011年6月27日|細石神社}} {{copyrights}} {{神社| 名称= 細石神社 | 所在地=福岡県糸島市三雲432| 祭神=磐長姫命
木之花開耶姫命| 社格=村社| 例祭=10月25日 例大祭}} '''細石神社'''（さざれいしじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[糸島市]]にある神社。古くは''佐々禮石神社''と表記されていた。祭神は[[イワナガヒメ|磐長姫]]と[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花開耶姫]]の姉妹二柱。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は村社。 == 概要 == [[伊都国]]の中心部に所在すると推察されている。 [[元禄]]8年（[[1695年]]）の「細石神社御縁起」では、古くは神田も多く大社であった。しかし、たびたび兵乱に見舞われ社殿を焼失した。さらに[[天正]]15年（[[1587年]]）の[[豊臣秀吉]]の[[太閤検地]]により、神田没収に遭い衰退した。現在は「村社、細石神社」の石柱が鳥居の脇に建てられている。 == 考察 == '''細石神社'''は社殿の背面にある「[[三雲南小路遺跡]]（[[文政]]5年（[[1822年]]）発見）の拝殿」であろうと地元民は云っている。 この「細石神社」の祭神は「[[磐長姫]]、[[木花開耶姫]]」である。つまり主祭神は「女神二柱」である。この事からと、昔は「[[高祖神社]]から神輿が細石神社に下る(上る?)」神幸があったという事から、この「細石神社」は「三代実録」に記載された「[[元慶]]元年（[[877年]]）、'''高磯ヒメ神'''に従五位下を授ける」とある「高磯ヒメ神」であると考えられると'''三雲の氏子'''は云っている。 ちなみに通説で云う「[[高祖神社]]の祭神が高磯ヒメ神であろう」というのは「[[高祖神社]]の主祭神、[[ホオリ|彦火火出見尊]]」を侮辱する事になることをここに記す。 この「細石神社」は「[[三雲南小路遺跡]]の、[[ニニギ|日子番ノニニギ命]]と木之花咲耶姫の墓の拝殿」であると考えられる。 地元考古学者[[原田大六]]は、[[三雲南小路遺跡]]発掘調査の時に「1号甕棺墓は武器類が多く、2号甕棺墓は玉類が多い。そして、この二つの甕棺は寄り添うようにして埋葬されている事から、これは'''男と女の墓'''である。つまり、'''王と妃の墓'''である」とコメントしている。 ==交通== [[九州旅客鉄道|JR]][[筑肥線]][[波多江駅]]から井原山行バス約10分。三雲宮(細石神社)前下車。 == 参考文献 == *『怡土志摩地理全誌』由比章祐/著 *『鎮守の社』糸島神職会 ==外部リンク== *[http://blog.goo.ne.jp/noyamany/e/e9d92be44b6b385a34c4830c7616596b 細石神社（福岡県前原市） - 九州神社紀行−ブログ（個人サイト） ] {{DEFAULTSORT: さされいししんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:糸島市の神社]] [[Category:糸島市]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称= 細石神社 | 所在地=福岡県糸島市三雲432| 祭神=磐長姫命
木之花開耶姫命| 社格=村社| 例祭=10月25日 例大祭}} '''細石神社'''（さざれいしじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[糸島市]]にある神社。古くは''佐々禮石神社''と表記されていた。祭神は[[イワナガヒメ|磐長姫]]と[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花開耶姫]]の姉妹二柱。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は村社。 == 概要 == [[伊都国]]の中心部に所在すると推察されている。 [[元禄]]8年（[[1695年]]）の「細石神社御縁起」では、古くは神田も多く大社であった。しかし、たびたび兵乱に見舞われ社殿を焼失した。さらに[[天正]]15年（[[1587年]]）の[[豊臣秀吉]]の[[太閤検地]]により、神田没収に遭い衰退した。現在は「村社、細石神社」の石柱が鳥居の脇に建てられている。 == 考察 == '''細石神社'''は社殿の背面にある「[[三雲南小路遺跡]]（[[文政]]5年（[[1822年]]）発見）の拝殿」であろうと地元民は云っている。 この「細石神社」の祭神は「[[磐長姫]]、[[木花開耶姫]]」である。つまり主祭神は「女神二柱」である。この事からと、昔は「[[高祖神社]]から神輿が細石神社に下る(上る?)」神幸があったという事から、この「細石神社」は「三代実録」に記載された「[[元慶]]元年（[[877年]]）、'''高磯ヒメ神'''に従五位下を授ける」とある「高磯ヒメ神」であると考えられると'''三雲の氏子'''は云っている。 ちなみに通説で云う「[[高祖神社]]の祭神が高磯ヒメ神であろう」というのは「[[高祖神社]]の主祭神、[[ホオリ|彦火火出見尊]]」を侮辱する事になることをここに記す。 この「細石神社」は「[[三雲南小路遺跡]]の、[[ニニギ|日子番ノニニギ命]]と木之花咲耶姫の墓の拝殿」であると考えられる。 地元考古学者[[原田大六]]は、[[三雲南小路遺跡]]発掘調査の時に「1号甕棺墓は武器類が多く、2号甕棺墓は玉類が多い。そして、この二つの甕棺は寄り添うようにして埋葬されている事から、これは'''男と女の墓'''である。つまり、'''王と妃の墓'''である」とコメントしている。 ==交通== [[九州旅客鉄道|JR]][[筑肥線]][[波多江駅]]から井原山行バス約10分。三雲宮(細石神社)前下車。 == 参考文献 == *『怡土志摩地理全誌』由比章祐/著 *『鎮守の社』糸島神職会 {{DEFAULTSORT: さされいししんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:糸島市]] [[Category:福岡県の旧村社]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
{{神社| 名称= 細石神社| 画像 = [[File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|300px|細石神社]]| 画像キャプション = 細石神社拝殿| 所在地=福岡県糸島市三雲432| 祭神=磐長姫命
木之花開耶姫命| 社格=村社| 例祭=10月25日 例大祭}} '''細石神社'''（さざれいしじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[糸島市]]にある神社。古くは「'''佐々禮石神社'''」と表記されていた。祭神は[[イワナガヒメ|磐長姫]]と[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花開耶姫]]の姉妹二柱。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は村社。 == 概要 == [[伊都国]]の中心部に所在すると推察されている。 [[元禄]]8年（[[1695年]]）の「細石神社御縁起」では、古くは神田も多く大社であった。しかし、たびたび兵乱に見舞われ社殿を焼失した。さらに[[天正]]15年（[[1587年]]）の[[豊臣秀吉]]の[[太閤検地]]により、神田没収に遭い衰退した。現在は「村社、細石神社」の石柱が鳥居の脇に建てられている。 == 考察 == '''細石神社'''は社殿の背面にある「[[三雲南小路遺跡]]（[[文政]]5年（[[1822年]]）発見）の拝殿」であろうと地元民は云っている。 この「細石神社」の祭神は「[[磐長姫]]、[[木花開耶姫]]」である。つまり主祭神は「女神二柱」である。この事からと、昔は「[[高祖神社]]から神輿が細石神社に下る(上る?)」神幸があったという事から、この「細石神社」は「三代実録」に記載された「[[元慶]]元年（[[877年]]）、'''高磯ヒメ神'''に従五位下を授ける」とある「高磯ヒメ神」であると考えられると'''三雲の氏子'''は云っている。 ちなみに通説で云う「[[高祖神社]]の祭神が高磯ヒメ神であろう」というのは「[[高祖神社]]の主祭神、[[ホオリ|彦火火出見尊]]」を侮辱する事になることをここに記す。 この「細石神社」は「[[三雲南小路遺跡]]の、[[ニニギ|日子番ノニニギ命]]と木之花咲耶姫の墓の拝殿」であると考えられる。 地元考古学者[[原田大六]]は、[[三雲南小路遺跡]]発掘調査の時に「1号甕棺墓は武器類が多く、2号甕棺墓は玉類が多い。そして、この二つの甕棺は寄り添うようにして埋葬されている事から、これは'''男と女の墓'''である。つまり、'''王と妃の墓'''である」とコメントしている。 ==交通== [[九州旅客鉄道|JR]][[筑肥線]][[波多江駅]]から井原山行バス約10分。三雲宮(細石神社)前下車。 == 参考文献 == *『怡土志摩地理全誌』由比章祐/著 *『鎮守の社』糸島神職会 {{DEFAULTSORT: さされいししんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:糸島市]] [[Category:福岡県の旧村社]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: 位置情報を追加しました。

木之花開耶姫命| 社格=村社| 例祭=10月25日 例大祭}} '''細石神社'''（さざれいしじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[糸島市]]にある神社。古くは「'''佐々禮石神社'''」と表記されていた。祭神は[[イワナガヒメ|磐長姫]]と[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花開耶姫]]の姉妹二柱。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は村社。 == 概要 == [[伊都国]]の中心部に所在すると推察されている。 [[元禄]]8年（[[1695年]]）の「細石神社御縁起」では、古くは神田も多く大社であった。しかし、たびたび兵乱に見舞われ社殿を焼失した。さらに[[天正]]15年（[[1587年]]）の[[豊臣秀吉]]の[[太閤検地]]により、神田没収に遭い衰退した。現在は「村社、細石神社」の石柱が鳥居の脇に建てられている。 == 考察 == '''細石神社'''は社殿の背面にある「[[三雲南小路遺跡]]（[[文政]]5年（[[1822年]]）発見）の拝殿」であろうと地元民は云っている。 この「細石神社」の祭神は「[[磐長姫]]、[[木花開耶姫]]」である。つまり主祭神は「女神二柱」である。この事からと、昔は「[[高祖神社]]から神輿が細石神社に下る(上る?)」神幸があったという事から、この「細石神社」は「三代実録」に記載された「[[元慶]]元年（[[877年]]）、'''高磯ヒメ神'''に従五位下を授ける」とある「高磯ヒメ神」であると考えられると'''三雲の氏子'''は云っている。 ちなみに通説で云う「[[高祖神社]]の祭神が高磯ヒメ神であろう」というのは「[[高祖神社]]の主祭神、[[ホオリ|彦火火出見尊]]」を侮辱する事になることをここに記す。 この「細石神社」は「[[三雲南小路遺跡]]の、[[ニニギ|日子番ノニニギ命]]と木之花咲耶姫の墓の拝殿」であると考えられる。 地元考古学者[[原田大六]]は、[[三雲南小路遺跡]]発掘調査の時に「1号甕棺墓は武器類が多く、2号甕棺墓は玉類が多い。そして、この二つの甕棺は寄り添うようにして埋葬されている事から、これは'''男と女の墓'''である。つまり、'''王と妃の墓'''である」とコメントしている。 ==交通== [[九州旅客鉄道|JR]][[筑肥線]][[波多江駅]]から井原山行バス約10分。三雲宮(細石神社)前下車。 == 参考文献 == *『怡土志摩地理全誌』由比章祐/著 *『鎮守の社』糸島神職会 {{DEFAULTSORT: さされいししんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:糸島市]] [[Category:福岡県の旧村社]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: 独自研究であり、検証できない
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称= 細石神社| 画像 = [[File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|300px|細石神社]]| 画像キャプション = 細石神社拝殿| 所在地=福岡県糸島市三雲432| 緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 32|緯度秒 = 13.18| 経度度 =130|経度分 = 14|経度秒 = 33.04| 祭神=磐長姫命

{{神社| 名称= 細石神社| 画像 = [[File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|300px|細石神社]]| 画像キャプション = 細石神社拝殿| 所在地=福岡県糸島市三雲432| 緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 32|緯度秒 = 13.18| 経度度 =130|経度分 = 14|経度秒 = 33.04| 祭神=磐長姫命
木之花開耶姫命| 社格=村社| 例祭=10月25日 例大祭}} '''細石神社'''（さざれいしじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[糸島市]]にある神社。古くは「'''佐々禮石神社'''」と表記されていた。祭神は[[イワナガヒメ|磐長姫]]と[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花開耶姫]]の姉妹二柱。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は村社。 == 概要 == [[伊都国]]の中心部に所在すると推察されている。 [[元禄]]8年（[[1695年]]）の「細石神社御縁起」では、古くは神田も多く大社であった。しかし、たびたび兵乱に見舞われ社殿を焼失した。さらに[[天正]]15年（[[1587年]]）の[[豊臣秀吉]]の[[太閤検地]]により、神田没収に遭い衰退した。現在は「村社、細石神社」の石柱が鳥居の脇に建てられている。 == 考察 == {{独自研究|section=1|date=2015年8月}} '''細石神社'''は社殿の背面にある「[[三雲南小路遺跡]]（[[文政]]5年（[[1822年]]）発見）の拝殿」であろうと地元民は云っている。 この「細石神社」の祭神は「[[磐長姫]]、[[木花開耶姫]]」である。つまり主祭神は「女神二柱」である。この事からと、昔は「[[高祖神社]]から神輿が細石神社に下る(上る?)」神幸があったという事から、この「細石神社」は「三代実録」に記載された「[[元慶]]元年（[[877年]]）、'''高磯ヒメ神'''に従五位下を授ける」とある「高磯ヒメ神」であると考えられると'''三雲の氏子'''は云っている。 ちなみに通説で云う「[[高祖神社]]の祭神が高磯ヒメ神であろう」というのは「[[高祖神社]]の主祭神、[[ホオリ|彦火火出見尊]]」を侮辱する事になることをここに記す。 この「細石神社」は「[[三雲南小路遺跡]]の、[[ニニギ|日子番ノニニギ命]]と木之花咲耶姫の墓の拝殿」であると考えられる。 地元考古学者[[原田大六]]は、[[三雲南小路遺跡]]発掘調査の時に「1号甕棺墓は武器類が多く、2号甕棺墓は玉類が多い。そして、この二つの甕棺は寄り添うようにして埋葬されている事から、これは'''男と女の墓'''である。つまり、'''王と妃の墓'''である」とコメントしている。 ==交通== [[九州旅客鉄道|JR]][[筑肥線]][[波多江駅]]から井原山行バス約10分。三雲宮(細石神社)前下車。 == 参考文献 == *『怡土志摩地理全誌』由比章祐/著 *『鎮守の社』糸島神職会 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さされいししんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:糸島市の歴史]] [[Category:福岡県の旧村社]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Shrine | Name = Sazareishi Jinja (細石神社) | Image = {{ill|File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|ja|Sazareishi Jinja|lt=300px|lt_ja=細石神社}} | Image Caption = Sazareishi Shrine Haiden (細石神社拝殿) | Location = 432, Mikumo, Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture | Latitude Degrees = 33 | Latitude Minutes = 32 | Latitude Seconds = 13.18 | Longitude Degrees = 130 | Longitude Minutes = 14 | Longitude Seconds = 33.04 | Map = Japan Fukuoka | Deities = Iwanagahi..."
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Sazareishi Jinja (細石神社) | Image = {{ill|File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|ja|Sazareishi Jinja|lt=300px|lt_ja=細石神社}} | Image Caption = Sazareishi Shrine Haiden (細石神社拝殿) | Location = 432, Mikumo, Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture | Latitude Degrees = 33 | Latitude Minutes = 32 | Latitude Seconds = 13.18 | Longitude Degrees = 130 | Longitude Minutes = 14 | Longitude Seconds = 33.04 | Map = Japan Fukuoka | Deities = Iwanagahime-no-mikoto (磐長姫命)
Konohananosakuyahime-no-mikoto (木之花開耶姫命) | Status = Village Shrine | Annual Festival = October 25, Annual Grand Festival }} '''Sazareishi Jinja''' (細石神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}} {{ill|Itoshima City|ja|糸島市}}. In ancient times, it was written as "'''Sasareishi Shrine'''". The enshrined deities are the two sister kami, {{ill|Iwanagahime|ja|イワナガヒメ|lt=Iwanagahime|lt_ja=磐長姫}} and {{ill|Konohananosakuyahime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ|lt=Konohananosakuyahime|lt_ja=木花開耶姫}}. According to the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度|lt=Former Shrine Rank|lt_ja=旧社格}}, its status is Village Shrine. == Overview == It is presumed to be located in the central part of {{ill|Itokuni|ja|伊都国}}. == Access == It is about a 10‑minute bus ride bound for Iharayama from {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|ja|九州旅客鉄道|lt=JR|lt_ja=JR}} {{ill|Chikuhi Line|ja|筑肥線}} {{ill|Hatae Station|ja|波多江駅}}, alighting at Mikumo Shrine (Sazareishi Jinja) bus stop. == References == * ''Itoshima Shima Chiri Zenshi'' by Yui Shōyū * ''Shrine of the Guardian Deity'' by the Itoshima Shinto Clergy Association {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さされいししんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Itoshima City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Itoshima City]] [[category:completion tier 1]][[ja:細石神社]]

According to the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度|lt=Former Shrine Rank|lt_ja=旧社格}}, its status is Village Shrine. == Overview == It is presumed to be located in the central part of {{ill|Itokuni|ja|伊都国}}. == Access == It is about a 10‑minute bus ride bound for Iharayama from {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|ja|九州旅客鉄道|lt=JR|lt_ja=JR}} {{ill|Chikuhi Line|ja|筑肥線}} {{ill|Hatae Station|ja|波多江駅}}, alighting at Mikumo Shrine (Sazareishi Jinja) bus stop. == References == * ''Itoshima Shima Chiri Zenshi'' by Yui Shōyū * ''Shrine of the Guardian Deity'' by the Itoshima Shinto Clergy Association {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さされいししんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Itoshima City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Itoshima City]] [[category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Iwanaga Faith]]{{Iwanaga Faith}}[[Category:Iwanaga Faith]] [[ja:細石神社]]

Konohananosakuyahime-no-mikoto (木之花開耶姫命) | Status = Village Shrine | Annual Festival = October 25, Annual Grand Festival }} '''Sazareishi Jinja''' (細石神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft}} {{ill|Itoshima City|ja|糸島市|12=draft}}. In ancient times, it was written as "'''Sasareishi Shrine'''". The enshrined deities are the two sister kami, {{ill|Iwanagahime|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanagahime|lt_ja=磐長姫|12=draft}} and {{ill|Konohananosakuyahime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ|lt=Konohananosakuyahime|lt_ja=木花開耶姫|12=draft}}. According to the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度|lt=Former Shrine Rank|lt_ja=旧社格|12=draft}}, its status is Village Shrine. == Overview == It is presumed to be located in the central part of {{ill|Itokuni|ja|伊都国|1=Itokoku|12=draft}}. == Access == It is about a 10‑minute bus ride bound for Iharayama from {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|ja|九州旅客鉄道|lt=JR|lt_ja=JR|12=draft}} {{ill|Chikuhi Line|ja|筑肥線|12=draft}} {{ill|Hatae Station|ja|波多江駅|12=draft}}, alighting at Mikumo Shrine (Sazareishi Jinja) bus stop. == References == * ''Itoshima Shima Chiri Zenshi'' by Yui Shōyū * ''Shrine of the Guardian Deity'' by the Itoshima Shinto Clergy Association {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Itoshima City]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Itoshima City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Iwanaga Faith]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Sazareishi Shrine[[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:細石神社]] [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:さされいししんしや}}

revision.comment: 34 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:細石神社 up to rev 74993435
revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称= 細石神社 | 画像 = [[File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|300px|細石神社]] | 画像キャプション = 細石神社拝殿 | 所在地=福岡県糸島市三雲432 | 緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 32|緯度秒 = 13.18 | 経度度 =130|経度分 = 14|経度秒 = 33.04 | 地図=Japan Fukuoka | 祭神=磐長姫命
木之花開耶姫命 | 社格=村社 | 例祭=10月25日 例大祭 }} '''細石神社'''（さざれいしじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[糸島市]]にある神社。古くは「'''佐々禮石神社'''」と表記されていた。祭神は[[イワナガヒメ|磐長姫]]と[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花開耶姫]]の姉妹二柱。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は村社。 == 概要 == [[伊都国]]の中心部に所在すると推察されている。 [[元禄]]8年（[[1695年]]）の「細石神社御縁起」では、古くは神田も多く大社であった。しかし、たびたび兵乱に見舞われ社殿を焼失した。さらに[[天正]]15年（[[1587年]]）の[[豊臣秀吉]]の[[太閤検地]]により、神田没収に遭い衰退した。現在は「村社、細石神社」の石柱が鳥居の脇に建てられている。 == 交通 == [[九州旅客鉄道|JR]][[筑肥線]][[波多江駅]]から井原山行バス約10分。三雲宮(細石神社)前下車。 == 参考文献 == *『怡土志摩地理全誌』由比章祐/著 *『鎮守の社』糸島神職会 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さされいししんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:糸島市の歴史]] [[Category:福岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:糸島市の建築物]]

{{Shrine | Name = Sazareishi Jinja (細石神社) | image = {{ill|File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|ja|Sazareishi Jinja|lt=300px|lt_ja=細石神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|qq=}} | Image Caption = Sazareishi Shrine Haiden (細石神社拝殿) | Location = 432, Mikumo, Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture | Latitude Degrees = 33 | Latitude Minutes = 32 | Latitude Seconds = 13.18 | Longitude Degrees = 130 | Longitude Minutes = 14 | Longitude Seconds = 33.04 | Map = Japan Fukuoka | Deities = Iwanagahime-no-mikoto (磐長姫命)
Konohananosakuyahime-no-mikoto (木之花開耶姫命) | Status = Village Shrine | Annual Festival = October 25, Annual Grand Festival }} '''Sazareishi Jinja''' (細石神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Itoshima City|ja|糸島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itoshima City|qq=}}. In ancient times, it was written as "'''Sasareishi Shrine'''". The enshrined deities are the two sister kami, {{ill|Iwanagahime|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanagahime|lt_ja=磐長姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanagahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|qq=}} and {{ill|Konohananosakuyahime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ|lt=Konohananosakuyahime|lt_ja=木花開耶姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohananosakuyahime|qq=}}. According to the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Former Shrine Rank|lt_ja=旧社格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Shrine Ranking System|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}}, its status is Village Shrine. == Overview == It is presumed to be located in the central part of {{ill|Itokuni|ja|伊都国|1=Itokoku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Itokoku|qq=}}. == Access == It is about a 10‑minute bus ride bound for Iharayama from {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|ja|九州旅客鉄道|lt=JR|lt_ja=JR|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu Railway Company|qq=}} {{ill|Chikuhi Line|ja|筑肥線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuhi Line|qq=}} {{ill|Hatae Station|ja|波多江駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatae Station|qq=}}, alighting at Mikumo Shrine (Sazareishi Jinja) bus stop. ==Overview== {{infobox religious building | name = Saiseki Shrine|native_name= 細石神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|300px|細石神社]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|imageキャプション = 細石神社拝殿 | address=福岡県糸島市三雲432 | 緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 32|緯度秒 = 13.18 | 経度度 =130|経度分 = 14|経度秒 = 33.04 | 地図=Japan Fukuoka | 祭神=磐長姫命
木之花開耶姫命 | 社格=村社 | 例祭=10月25日 例大祭 }} '''Sazareishi Jinja''' (細石神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Itoshima|ja|糸島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itoshima|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}. Historically referred to as "Sasareiishi Jinja" (佐々禮石神社), the enshrined deities are the sister goddesses {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|磐長姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|qq=}} and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}. It was classified as a {{ill|Village shrine (Shinto)|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Shinto)|qq=}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}}. == Overview == The shrine is presumed to be situated in the central area of the ancient {{ill|Ito Kingdom|ja|伊都国|1=Itokoku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ito Kingdom|13=User:Immanuelle/Itokoku|qq=}}. According to the shrine history titled "Sazareishi Jinja Goengi," compiled in the {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genroku|qq=}} era (1695), the shrine was once a large and significant establishment with extensive sacred rice fields. However, repeated conflicts resulted in the destruction of the shrine's buildings. Furthermore, during the {{ill|Taikō land survey|ja|太閤検地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taikō land survey|qq=}} conducted by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}} in the {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō (Momoyama period)|qq=}} era (1587), the shrine's sacred lands were confiscated, leading to its decline. Today, a stone pillar labeled "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Jinja" stands next to the torii gate. == Access == The shrine can be reached by taking a bus bound for Iharayama from {{ill|JR Kyushu|ja|九州旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Kyushu|qq=}}'s {{ill|Chikuhi Line|ja|筑肥線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuhi Line|qq=}} at {{ill|Hatae Station|ja|波多江駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatae Station|qq=}}. Disembark at Mikumomiya (Sazareishi Jinja-mae) after approximately 10 minutes. == References == * "Itoshima Geographical Encyclopedia" by Shusuke Yui. * "Shrines of Itoshima" by the Itoshima Shinto Priest Association. {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Itoshima]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Itoshima]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Saisa Shrine]] }} [[sn:Saiseki Shrine]] [[en:Saiseki Shrine]] [[ja:細石神社]] == References == * ''Itoshima Shima Chiri Zenshi'' by Yui Shōyū * ''Shrine of the Guardian Deity'' by the Itoshima Shinto Clergy Association {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Itoshima City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Itoshima City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Iwanaga Faith]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:細石神社]]

{{Shrine | Name = Sazareishi Jinja (細石神社) | image = {{ill|File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|ja|Sazareishi Jinja|lt=300px|lt_ja=細石神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|qq=}} | Image Caption = Sazareishi Shrine Haiden (細石神社拝殿) | Location = 432, Mikumo, Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture | Latitude Degrees = 33 | Latitude Minutes = 32 | Latitude Seconds = 13.18 | Longitude Degrees = 130 | Longitude Minutes = 14 | Longitude Seconds = 33.04 | Map = Japan Fukuoka | Deities = Iwanagahime-no-mikoto (磐長姫命)
Konohananosakuyahime-no-mikoto (木之花開耶姫命) | Status = Village Shrine | Annual Festival = October 25, Annual Grand Festival }} '''Sazareishi Jinja''' (細石神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Itoshima City|ja|糸島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itoshima City|qq=}}. In ancient times, it was written as "'''Sasareishi Shrine'''". The enshrined deities are the two sister kami, {{ill|Iwanagahime|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanagahime|lt_ja=磐長姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanagahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|qq=}} and {{ill|Konohananosakuyahime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ|lt=Konohananosakuyahime|lt_ja=木花開耶姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohananosakuyahime|qq=}}. According to the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Former Shrine Rank|lt_ja=旧社格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Shrine Ranking System|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}}, its status is Village Shrine. == Overview == It is presumed to be located in the central part of {{ill|Itokuni|ja|伊都国|1=Itokoku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Itokoku|qq=}}. == Access == It is about a 10‑minute bus ride bound for Iharayama from {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|ja|九州旅客鉄道|lt=JR|lt_ja=JR|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu Railway Company|qq=}} {{ill|Chikuhi Line|ja|筑肥線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuhi Line|qq=}} {{ill|Hatae Station|ja|波多江駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatae Station|qq=}}, alighting at Mikumo Shrine (Sazareishi Jinja) bus stop. ==Overview== {{infobox religious building | name = Saiseki Shrine|native_name= 細石神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|300px|細石神社]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|imageキャプション = 細石神社拝殿 | address=福岡県糸島市三雲432 | 緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 32|緯度秒 = 13.18 | 経度度 =130|経度分 = 14|経度秒 = 33.04 | 地図=Japan Fukuoka | 祭神=磐長姫命
木之花開耶姫命 | 社格=村社 | 例祭=10月25日 例大祭 }} '''Sazareishi Jinja''' (細石神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Itoshima|ja|糸島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itoshima|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}. Historically referred to as "Sasareiishi Jinja" (佐々禮石神社), the enshrined deities are the sister goddesses {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|磐長姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|qq=}} and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}. It was classified as a {{ill|Village shrine (Shinto)|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Shinto)|qq=}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}}. == Overview == The shrine is presumed to be situated in the central area of the ancient {{ill|Ito Kingdom|ja|伊都国|1=Itokoku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ito Kingdom|13=User:Immanuelle/Itokoku|qq=}}. According to the shrine history titled "Sazareishi Jinja Goengi," compiled in the {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genroku|qq=}} era (1695), the shrine was once a large and significant establishment with extensive sacred rice fields. However, repeated conflicts resulted in the destruction of the shrine's buildings. Furthermore, during the {{ill|Taikō land survey|ja|太閤検地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taikō land survey|qq=}} conducted by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}} in the {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō (Momoyama period)|qq=}} era (1587), the shrine's sacred lands were confiscated, leading to its decline. Today, a stone pillar labeled "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Jinja" stands next to the torii gate. == Access == The shrine can be reached by taking a bus bound for Iharayama from {{ill|JR Kyushu|ja|九州旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Kyushu|qq=}}'s {{ill|Chikuhi Line|ja|筑肥線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuhi Line|qq=}} at {{ill|Hatae Station|ja|波多江駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatae Station|qq=}}. Disembark at Mikumomiya (Sazareishi Jinja-mae) after approximately 10 minutes. ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name= Sazareishi Shrine | image = [[File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|300px|Sazareishi Shrine]] | image Caption = Worship Hall of Sazareishi Shrine | location=Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | Latitude = 33|Latitude_min = 32|Latitude_sec = 13.18 | Longitude =130|Longitude_min = 14|Longitude_sec = 33.04 | Map=Japan Fukuoka | deity=Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto
Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto | Shrine Rank=Village Shrine | Annual Festival=October 25 }} '''Sazareishi Shrine''' (細石神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. Historically, it was written as "'''Sasareishi Shrine'''" (佐々禮石神社). The enshrined deities are the sister goddesses [[Iwanaga-hime]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|former shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a village shrine. == Overview == The shrine is believed to be located in the central area of the ancient [[Itokoku]] region. According to the "Sazareishi Shrine Origin Record" from the 8th year of [[Genroku]] (1695), the shrine once had many sacred rice fields and was a large shrine. However, it suffered from repeated military conflicts, leading to the destruction of its shrine buildings. Furthermore, during the [[Taikō land survey]] conducted by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 15th year of [[Tenshō]] (1587), the shrine's sacred rice fields were confiscated, causing its decline. Currently, a stone pillar inscribed with "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Shrine" stands beside the torii gate. == Access == Approximately 10 minutes by bus towards Iharayama from [[Hatae Station]] on the [[Chikuhi Line]] operated by [[JR Kyushu]]. Get off at Mikumo Shrine (Sazareishi Shrine) bus stop. == References == * ''Itoshima and Ido Geography Complete Encyclopedia'' by Shoyu Yui * ''Shrines of Guardian Deities'' by Itoshima Shinto Priest Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Sazare Ishi Shrine]] [[ja:%E7%B4%B0%E7%9F%B3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Sazare Ishi Shrine]] == References == * "Itoshima Geographical Encyclopedia" by Shusuke Yui. * "Shrines of Itoshima" by the Itoshima Shinto Priest Association. {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Itoshima]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Itoshima]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Saisa Shrine]] }} [[sn:Saiseki Shrine]] [[en:Saiseki Shrine]] [[ja:細石神社]] == References == * ''Itoshima Shima Chiri Zenshi'' by Yui Shōyū * ''Shrine of the Guardian Deity'' by the Itoshima Shinto Clergy Association {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Itoshima City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Itoshima City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Iwanaga Faith]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:細石神社]]

The enshrined deities are the two sister kami, {{ill|Iwanagahime|ja|イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|lt=Iwanagahime|lt_ja=磐長姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanagahime|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|qq=}} and {{ill|Konohananosakuyahime|ja|コノハナノサクヤビメ|lt=Konohananosakuyahime|lt_ja=木花開耶姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohananosakuyahime|qq=}}. According to the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Former Shrine Rank|lt_ja=旧社格|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Shrine Ranking System|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}}, its status is Village Shrine. == Overview == It is presumed to be located in the central part of {{ill|Itokuni|ja|伊都国|1=Itokoku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Itokoku|qq=}}. == Access == It is about a 10‑minute bus ride bound for Iharayama from {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|ja|九州旅客鉄道|lt=JR|lt_ja=JR|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyushu Railway Company|qq=}} {{ill|Chikuhi Line|ja|筑肥線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuhi Line|qq=}} {{ill|Hatae Station|ja|波多江駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatae Station|qq=}}, alighting at Mikumo Shrine (Sazareishi Jinja) bus stop. ==Overview== {{infobox religious building | name = Saiseki Shrine|native_name= 細石神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|300px|細石神社]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|imageキャプション = 細石神社拝殿 | address=福岡県糸島市三雲432 | 緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 32|緯度秒 = 13.18 | 経度度 =130|経度分 = 14|経度秒 = 33.04 | 地図=Japan Fukuoka | 祭神=磐長姫命
木之花開耶姫命 | 社格=村社 | 例祭=10月25日 例大祭 }} '''Sazareishi Jinja''' (細石神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Itoshima|ja|糸島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itoshima|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}. Historically referred to as "Sasareiishi Jinja" (佐々禮石神社), the enshrined deities are the sister goddesses {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|磐長姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|qq=}} and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}. It was classified as a {{ill|Village shrine (Shinto)|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Shinto)|qq=}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}}. == Overview == The shrine is presumed to be situated in the central area of the ancient {{ill|Ito Kingdom|ja|伊都国|1=Itokoku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ito Kingdom|13=User:Immanuelle/Itokoku|qq=}}. According to the shrine history titled "Sazareishi Jinja Goengi," compiled in the {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genroku|qq=}} era (1695), the shrine was once a large and significant establishment with extensive sacred rice fields. However, repeated conflicts resulted in the destruction of the shrine's buildings. Furthermore, during the {{ill|Taikō land survey|ja|太閤検地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taikō land survey|qq=}} conducted by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}} in the {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō (Momoyama period)|qq=}} era (1587), the shrine's sacred lands were confiscated, leading to its decline. Today, a stone pillar labeled "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Jinja" stands next to the torii gate. == Access == The shrine can be reached by taking a bus bound for Iharayama from {{ill|JR Kyushu|ja|九州旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Kyushu|qq=}}'s {{ill|Chikuhi Line|ja|筑肥線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuhi Line|qq=}} at {{ill|Hatae Station|ja|波多江駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatae Station|qq=}}. Disembark at Mikumomiya (Sazareishi Jinja-mae) after approximately 10 minutes. ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name= Sazareishi Shrine | image = [[File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|300px|Sazareishi Shrine]] | image Caption = Worship Hall of Sazareishi Shrine | location=Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | Latitude = 33|Latitude_min = 32|Latitude_sec = 13.18 | Longitude =130|Longitude_min = 14|Longitude_sec = 33.04 | Map=Japan Fukuoka | deity=Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto
Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto | Shrine Rank=Village Shrine | Annual Festival=October 25 }} '''Sazareishi Shrine''' (細石神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. Historically, it was written as "'''Sasareishi Shrine'''" (佐々禮石神社). The enshrined deities are the sister goddesses [[Iwanaga-hime]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|former shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a village shrine. == Overview == The shrine is believed to be located in the central area of the ancient [[Itokoku]] region. According to the "Sazareishi Shrine Origin Record" from the 8th year of [[Genroku]] (1695), the shrine once had many sacred rice fields and was a large shrine. However, it suffered from repeated military conflicts, leading to the destruction of its shrine buildings. Furthermore, during the [[Taikō land survey]] conducted by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 15th year of [[Tenshō]] (1587), the shrine's sacred rice fields were confiscated, causing its decline. Currently, a stone pillar inscribed with "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Shrine" stands beside the torii gate. == Access == Approximately 10 minutes by bus towards Iharayama from [[Hatae Station]] on the [[Chikuhi Line]] operated by [[JR Kyushu]]. Get off at Mikumo Shrine (Sazareishi Shrine) bus stop. == References == * ''Itoshima and Ido Geography Complete Encyclopedia'' by Shoyu Yui * ''Shrines of Guardian Deities'' by Itoshima Shinto Priest Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:%E7%B4%B0%E7%9F%B3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Sazareishi Shrine]] == References == * "Itoshima Geographical Encyclopedia" by Shusuke Yui. * "Shrines of Itoshima" by the Itoshima Shinto Priest Association. {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Itoshima]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Itoshima]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Saisa Shrine]] }} [[sn:Saiseki Shrine]] [[en:Saiseki Shrine]] [[ja:細石神社]] == References == * ''Itoshima Shima Chiri Zenshi'' by Yui Shōyū * ''Shrine of the Guardian Deity'' by the Itoshima Shinto Clergy Association {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Itoshima City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Itoshima City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Iwanaga Faith]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:細石神社]]

木之花開耶姫命 | 社格=村社 | 例祭=10月25日 例大祭 }} '''Sazareishi Jinja''' (細石神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Itoshima|ja|糸島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itoshima|qq=}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuoka Prefecture|qq=}}. Historically referred to as "Sasareiishi Jinja" (佐々禮石神社), the enshrined deities are the sister goddesses {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|磐長姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|qq=}} and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}. It was classified as a {{ill|Village shrine (Shinto)|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Shinto)|qq=}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}}. == Overview == The shrine is presumed to be situated in the central area of the ancient {{ill|Ito Kingdom|ja|伊都国|1=Itokoku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ito Kingdom|13=User:Immanuelle/Itokoku|qq=}}. According to the shrine history titled "Sazareishi Jinja Goengi," compiled in the {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genroku|qq=}} era (1695), the shrine was once a large and significant establishment with extensive sacred rice fields. However, repeated conflicts resulted in the destruction of the shrine's buildings. Furthermore, during the {{ill|Taikō land survey|ja|太閤検地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taikō land survey|qq=}} conducted by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|ja|豊臣秀吉|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qq=}} in the {{ill|Tenshō|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō (Momoyama period)|qq=}} era (1587), the shrine's sacred lands were confiscated, leading to its decline. Today, a stone pillar labeled "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Jinja" stands next to the torii gate. == Access == The shrine can be reached by taking a bus bound for Iharayama from {{ill|JR Kyushu|ja|九州旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Kyushu|qq=}}'s {{ill|Chikuhi Line|ja|筑肥線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuhi Line|qq=}} at {{ill|Hatae Station|ja|波多江駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatae Station|qq=}}. Disembark at Mikumomiya (Sazareishi Jinja-mae) after approximately 10 minutes. ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name= Sazareishi Shrine | image = [[File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|300px|Sazareishi Shrine]] | image Caption = Worship Hall of Sazareishi Shrine | location=Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | Latitude = 33|Latitude_min = 32|Latitude_sec = 13.18 | Longitude =130|Longitude_min = 14|Longitude_sec = 33.04 | Map=Japan Fukuoka | deity=Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto
Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto | Shrine Rank=Village Shrine | Annual Festival=October 25 }} '''Sazareishi Shrine''' (細石神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. Historically, it was written as "'''Sasareishi Shrine'''" (佐々禮石神社). The enshrined deities are the sister goddesses [[Iwanaga-hime]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|former shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a village shrine. == Overview == The shrine is believed to be located in the central area of the ancient [[Itokoku]] region. According to the "Sazareishi Shrine Origin Record" from the 8th year of [[Genroku]] (1695), the shrine once had many sacred rice fields and was a large shrine. However, it suffered from repeated military conflicts, leading to the destruction of its shrine buildings. Furthermore, during the [[Taikō land survey]] conducted by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 15th year of [[Tenshō]] (1587), the shrine's sacred rice fields were confiscated, causing its decline. Currently, a stone pillar inscribed with "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Shrine" stands beside the torii gate. == Access == Approximately 10 minutes by bus towards Iharayama from [[Hatae Station]] on the [[Chikuhi Line]] operated by [[JR Kyushu]]. Get off at Mikumo Shrine (Sazareishi Shrine) bus stop. == References == * ''Itoshima and Ido Geography Complete Encyclopedia'' by Shoyu Yui * ''Shrines of Guardian Deities'' by Itoshima Shinto Priest Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:%E7%B4%B0%E7%9F%B3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Sazareishi Shrine]] == References == * "Itoshima Geographical Encyclopedia" by Shusuke Yui. * "Shrines of Itoshima" by the Itoshima Shinto Priest Association. {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Itoshima]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Itoshima]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Saisa Shrine]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[en:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:細石神社]] == References == * ''Itoshima Shima Chiri Zenshi'' by Yui Shōyū * ''Shrine of the Guardian Deity'' by the Itoshima Shinto Clergy Association {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Itoshima City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Itoshima City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Iwanaga Faith]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:細石神社]]

Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto | Shrine Rank=Village Shrine | Annual Festival=October 25 }} '''Sazareishi Shrine''' (細石神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. Historically, it was written as "'''Sasareishi Shrine'''" (佐々禮石神社). The enshrined deities are the sister goddesses [[Iwanaga-hime]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|former shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a village shrine. == Overview == The shrine is believed to be located in the central area of the ancient [[Itokoku]] region. According to the "Sazareishi Shrine Origin Record" from the 8th year of [[Genroku]] (1695), the shrine once had many sacred rice fields and was a large shrine. However, it suffered from repeated military conflicts, leading to the destruction of its shrine buildings. Furthermore, during the [[Taikō land survey]] conducted by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 15th year of [[Tenshō]] (1587), the shrine's sacred rice fields were confiscated, causing its decline. Currently, a stone pillar inscribed with "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Shrine" stands beside the torii gate. == Access == Approximately 10 minutes by bus towards Iharayama from [[Hatae Station]] on the [[Chikuhi Line]] operated by [[JR Kyushu]]. Get off at Mikumo Shrine (Sazareishi Shrine) bus stop. == References == * ''Itoshima and Ido Geography Complete Encyclopedia'' by Shoyu Yui * ''Shrines of Guardian Deities'' by Itoshima Shinto Priest Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:%E7%B4%B0%E7%9F%B3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Sazareishi Shrine]] == References == * "Itoshima Geographical Encyclopedia" by Shusuke Yui. * "Shrines of Itoshima" by the Itoshima Shinto Priest Association. {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Itoshima]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Itoshima]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[en:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:細石神社]] == References == * ''Itoshima Shima Chiri Zenshi'' by Yui Shōyū * ''Shrine of the Guardian Deity'' by the Itoshima Shinto Clergy Association {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Itoshima City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Itoshima City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Iwanaga Faith]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:細石神社]]

Historically referred to as "Sasareiishi Jinja" (佐々禮石神社), the enshrined deities are the sister goddesses {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|磐長姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|qq=}} and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}. It was classified as a {{ill|Village shrine (Shinto)|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Shinto)|qq=}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Overview == The shrine is presumed to be situated in the central area of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]]. According to the shrine history titled "Sazareishi Jinja Goengi," compiled in the [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] era (1695), the shrine was once a large and significant establishment with extensive sacred rice fields. However, repeated conflicts resulted in the destruction of the shrine's buildings. Furthermore, during the {{ill|Taikō land survey|ja|太閤検地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taikō land survey|qq=}} conducted by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] era (1587), the shrine's sacred lands were confiscated, leading to its decline. Today, a stone pillar labeled "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Jinja" stands next to the torii gate. == Access == The shrine can be reached by taking a bus bound for Iharayama from [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|JR Kyushu]]'s [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] at [[:en:Hatae Station|Hatae Station]]. Disembark at Mikumomiya (Sazareishi Jinja-mae) after approximately 10 minutes. ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name= Sazareishi Shrine | image = [[File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|300px|Sazareishi Shrine]] | image Caption = Worship Hall of Sazareishi Shrine | location=Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | Latitude = 33|Latitude_min = 32|Latitude_sec = 13.18 | Longitude =130|Longitude_min = 14|Longitude_sec = 33.04 | Map=Japan Fukuoka | deity=Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto
Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto | Shrine Rank=Village Shrine | Annual Festival=October 25 }} '''Sazareishi Shrine''' (細石神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. Historically, it was written as "'''Sasareishi Shrine'''" (佐々禮石神社). The enshrined deities are the sister goddesses [[Iwanaga-hime]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|former shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a village shrine. == Overview == The shrine is believed to be located in the central area of the ancient [[Itokoku]] region. According to the "Sazareishi Shrine Origin Record" from the 8th year of [[Genroku]] (1695), the shrine once had many sacred rice fields and was a large shrine. However, it suffered from repeated military conflicts, leading to the destruction of its shrine buildings. Furthermore, during the [[Taikō land survey]] conducted by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 15th year of [[Tenshō]] (1587), the shrine's sacred rice fields were confiscated, causing its decline. Currently, a stone pillar inscribed with "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Shrine" stands beside the torii gate. == Access == Approximately 10 minutes by bus towards Iharayama from [[Hatae Station]] on the [[Chikuhi Line]] operated by [[JR Kyushu]]. Get off at Mikumo Shrine (Sazareishi Shrine) bus stop. == References == * ''Itoshima and Ido Geography Complete Encyclopedia'' by Shoyu Yui * ''Shrines of Guardian Deities'' by Itoshima Shinto Priest Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:%E7%B4%B0%E7%9F%B3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Sazareishi Shrine]] == References == * "Itoshima Geographical Encyclopedia" by Shusuke Yui. * "Shrines of Itoshima" by the Itoshima Shinto Priest Association. {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Itoshima]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Itoshima]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[en:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:細石神社]] == References == * ''Itoshima Shima Chiri Zenshi'' by Yui Shōyū * ''Shrine of the Guardian Deity'' by the Itoshima Shinto Clergy Association {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Itoshima City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Itoshima City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Iwanaga Faith]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:細石神社]] [[Category:糸島市の建築物]]

Historically referred to as "Sasareiishi Jinja" (佐々禮石神社), the enshrined deities are the sister goddesses {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|磐長姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|qq=}} and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}. It was classified as a {{ill|Village shrine (Shinto)|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Shinto)|qq=}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Overview == The shrine is presumed to be situated in the central area of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]]. According to the shrine history titled "Sazareishi Jinja Goengi," compiled in the [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] era (1695), the shrine was once a large and significant establishment with extensive sacred rice fields. However, repeated conflicts resulted in the destruction of the shrine's buildings. Furthermore, during the {{ill|Taikō land survey|ja|太閤検地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taikō land survey|qq=}} conducted by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] era (1587), the shrine's sacred lands were confiscated, leading to its decline. Today, a stone pillar labeled "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Jinja" stands next to the torii gate. == Access == The shrine can be reached by taking a bus bound for Iharayama from [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|JR Kyushu]]'s [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] at [[:en:Hatae Station|Hatae Station]]. Disembark at Mikumomiya (Sazareishi Jinja-mae) after approximately 10 minutes. ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name= Sazareishi Shrine | image = [[File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|300px|Sazareishi Shrine]] | image Caption = Worship Hall of Sazareishi Shrine | location=Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | Latitude = 33|Latitude_min = 32|Latitude_sec = 13.18 | Longitude =130|Longitude_min = 14|Longitude_sec = 33.04 | Map=Japan Fukuoka | deity=Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto
Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto | Shrine Rank=Village Shrine | Annual Festival=October 25 }} '''Sazareishi Shrine''' (細石神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. Historically, it was written as "'''Sasareishi Shrine'''" (佐々禮石神社). The enshrined deities are the sister goddesses [[Iwanaga-hime]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|former shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a village shrine. == Overview == The shrine is believed to be located in the central area of the ancient [[Itokoku]] region. According to the "Sazareishi Shrine Origin Record" from the 8th year of [[Genroku]] (1695), the shrine once had many sacred rice fields and was a large shrine. However, it suffered from repeated military conflicts, leading to the destruction of its shrine buildings. Furthermore, during the [[Taikō land survey]] conducted by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 15th year of [[Tenshō]] (1587), the shrine's sacred rice fields were confiscated, causing its decline. Currently, a stone pillar inscribed with "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Shrine" stands beside the torii gate. == Access == Approximately 10 minutes by bus towards Iharayama from [[Hatae Station]] on the [[Chikuhi Line]] operated by [[JR Kyushu]]. Get off at Mikumo Shrine (Sazareishi Shrine) bus stop. == References == * ''Itoshima and Ido Geography Complete Encyclopedia'' by Shoyu Yui * ''Shrines of Guardian Deities'' by Itoshima Shinto Priest Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:%E7%B4%B0%E7%9F%B3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] == References == * "Itoshima Geographical Encyclopedia" by Shusuke Yui. * "Shrines of Itoshima" by the Itoshima Shinto Priest Association. {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Itoshima]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Itoshima]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:細石神社]] == References == * ''Itoshima Shima Chiri Zenshi'' by Yui Shōyū * ''Shrine of the Guardian Deity'' by the Itoshima Shinto Clergy Association {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Itoshima City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Itoshima City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Iwanaga Faith]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:細石神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:糸島市の歴史]]

Historically referred to as "Sasareiishi Jinja" (佐々禮石神社), the enshrined deities are the sister goddesses {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|磐長姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワナガヒメ}} and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. It was classified as a {{ill|Village shrine (Shinto)|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Shinto)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Overview == The shrine is presumed to be situated in the central area of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]]. According to the shrine history titled "Sazareishi Jinja Goengi," compiled in the [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] era (1695), the shrine was once a large and significant establishment with extensive sacred rice fields. However, repeated conflicts resulted in the destruction of the shrine's buildings. Furthermore, during the {{ill|Taikō land survey|ja|太閤検地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taikō land survey|qq=}} conducted by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] era (1587), the shrine's sacred lands were confiscated, leading to its decline. Today, a stone pillar labeled "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Jinja" stands next to the torii gate. == Access == The shrine can be reached by taking a bus bound for Iharayama from [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|JR Kyushu]]'s [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] at [[:en:Hatae Station|Hatae Station]]. Disembark at Mikumomiya (Sazareishi Jinja-mae) after approximately 10 minutes. ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name= Sazareishi Shrine | image = [[File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|300px|Sazareishi Shrine]] | image Caption = Worship Hall of Sazareishi Shrine | location=Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | Latitude = 33|Latitude_min = 32|Latitude_sec = 13.18 | Longitude =130|Longitude_min = 14|Longitude_sec = 33.04 | Map=Japan Fukuoka | deity=Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto
Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto | Shrine Rank=Village Shrine | Annual Festival=October 25 }} '''Sazareishi Shrine''' (細石神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. Historically, it was written as "'''Sasareishi Shrine'''" (佐々禮石神社). The enshrined deities are the sister goddesses [[Iwanaga-hime]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|former shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a village shrine. == Overview == The shrine is believed to be located in the central area of the ancient [[Itokoku]] region. According to the "Sazareishi Shrine Origin Record" from the 8th year of [[Genroku]] (1695), the shrine once had many sacred rice fields and was a large shrine. However, it suffered from repeated military conflicts, leading to the destruction of its shrine buildings. Furthermore, during the [[Taikō land survey]] conducted by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 15th year of [[Tenshō]] (1587), the shrine's sacred rice fields were confiscated, causing its decline. Currently, a stone pillar inscribed with "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Shrine" stands beside the torii gate. == Access == Approximately 10 minutes by bus towards Iharayama from [[Hatae Station]] on the [[Chikuhi Line]] operated by [[JR Kyushu]]. Get off at Mikumo Shrine (Sazareishi Shrine) bus stop. == References == * ''Itoshima and Ido Geography Complete Encyclopedia'' by Shoyu Yui * ''Shrines of Guardian Deities'' by Itoshima Shinto Priest Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:%E7%B4%B0%E7%9F%B3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] == References == * "Itoshima Geographical Encyclopedia" by Shusuke Yui. * "Shrines of Itoshima" by the Itoshima Shinto Priest Association. {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Itoshima]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:細石神社]] == References == * ''Itoshima Shima Chiri Zenshi'' by Yui Shōyū * ''Shrine of the Guardian Deity'' by the Itoshima Shinto Clergy Association {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Itoshima City]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Itoshima City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Iwanaga Faith]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:細石神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:糸島市の歴史]] {{Commons category|Sazareishi-jinja}}

木之花開耶姫命 | 社格=村社 | 例祭=10月25日 例大祭 }} '''Sazareishi Jinja''' (細石神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. Historically referred to as "Sasareiishi Jinja" (佐々禮石神社), the enshrined deities are the sister goddesses {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|磐長姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワナガヒメ}} and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. It was classified as a {{ill|Village shrine (Shinto)|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Shinto)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Overview == The shrine is presumed to be situated in the central area of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]]. According to the shrine history titled "Sazareishi Jinja Goengi," compiled in the [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] era (1695), the shrine was once a large and significant establishment with extensive sacred rice fields. However, repeated conflicts resulted in the destruction of the shrine's buildings. Furthermore, during the {{ill|Taikō land survey|ja|太閤検地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taikō land survey|qq=}} conducted by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] era (1587), the shrine's sacred lands were confiscated, leading to its decline. Today, a stone pillar labeled "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Jinja" stands next to the torii gate. == Access == The shrine can be reached by taking a bus bound for Iharayama from [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|JR Kyushu]]'s [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] at [[:en:Hatae Station|Hatae Station]]. Disembark at Mikumomiya (Sazareishi Jinja-mae) after approximately 10 minutes. ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name= Sazareishi Shrine | image = [[File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|300px|Sazareishi Shrine]] | image Caption = Worship Hall of Sazareishi Shrine | location=Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | Latitude = 33|Latitude_min = 32|Latitude_sec = 13.18 | Longitude =130|Longitude_min = 14|Longitude_sec = 33.04 | Map=Japan Fukuoka | deity=Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto
Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto | Shrine Rank=Village Shrine | Annual Festival=October 25 }} '''Sazareishi Shrine''' (細石神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. Historically, it was written as "'''Sasareishi Shrine'''" (佐々禮石神社). The enshrined deities are the sister goddesses [[Iwanaga-hime]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|former shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a village shrine. == Overview == The shrine is believed to be located in the central area of the ancient [[Itokoku]] region. According to the "Sazareishi Shrine Origin Record" from the 8th year of [[Genroku]] (1695), the shrine once had many sacred rice fields and was a large shrine. However, it suffered from repeated military conflicts, leading to the destruction of its shrine buildings. Furthermore, during the [[Taikō land survey]] conducted by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 15th year of [[Tenshō]] (1587), the shrine's sacred rice fields were confiscated, causing its decline. Currently, a stone pillar inscribed with "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Shrine" stands beside the torii gate. == Access == Approximately 10 minutes by bus towards Iharayama from [[Hatae Station]] on the [[Chikuhi Line]] operated by [[JR Kyushu]]. 木之花開耶姫命 | 社格=村社 | 例祭=10月25日 例大祭 }} '''Sazareishi Jinja''' (細石神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. Historically referred to as "Sasareiishi Jinja" (佐々禮石神社), the enshrined deities are the sister goddesses {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|磐長姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワナガヒメ}} and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. It was classified as a {{ill|Village shrine (Shinto)|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Shinto)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Overview == The shrine is presumed to be situated in the central area of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]]. According to the shrine history titled "Sazareishi Jinja Goengi," compiled in the [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] era (1695), the shrine was once a large and significant establishment with extensive sacred rice fields. However, repeated conflicts resulted in the destruction of the shrine's buildings. Furthermore, during the {{ill|Taikō land survey|ja|太閤検地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taikō land survey|qq=}} conducted by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] era (1587), the shrine's sacred lands were confiscated, leading to its decline. Today, a stone pillar labeled "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Jinja" stands next to the torii gate. == Access == The shrine can be reached by taking a bus bound for Iharayama from [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|JR Kyushu]]'s [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] at [[:en:Hatae Station|Hatae Station]]. Disembark at Mikumomiya (Sazareishi Jinja-mae) after approximately 10 minutes. ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name= Sazareishi Shrine | image = [[File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|300px|Sazareishi Shrine]] | image Caption = Worship Hall of Sazareishi Shrine | location=Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | Latitude = 33|Latitude_min = 32|Latitude_sec = 13.18 | Longitude =130|Longitude_min = 14|Longitude_sec = 33.04 | Map=Japan Fukuoka | deity=Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto
Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto | Shrine Rank=Village Shrine | Annual Festival=October 25 }} '''Sazareishi Shrine''' (細石神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. Historically, it was written as "'''Sasareishi Shrine'''" (佐々禮石神社). The enshrined deities are the sister goddesses [[Iwanaga-hime]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|former shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a village shrine. == Overview == The shrine is believed to be located in the central area of the ancient [[Itokoku]] region. According to the "Sazareishi Shrine Origin Record" from the 8th year of [[Genroku]] (1695), the shrine once had many sacred rice fields and was a large shrine. However, it suffered from repeated military conflicts, leading to the destruction of its shrine buildings. Furthermore, during the [[Taikō land survey]] conducted by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 15th year of [[Tenshō]] (1587), the shrine's sacred rice fields were confiscated, causing its decline. Currently, a stone pillar inscribed with "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Shrine" stands beside the torii gate. == Access == Approximately 10 minutes by bus towards Iharayama from [[Hatae Station]] on the [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] operated by [[JR Kyushu]]. Get off at Mikumo Shrine (Sazareishi Shrine) bus stop. == References == * ''Itoshima and Ido Geography Complete Encyclopedia'' by Shoyu Yui * ''Shrines of Guardian Deities'' by Itoshima Shinto Priest Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:%E7%B4%B0%E7%9F%B3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] == References == * "Itoshima Geographical Encyclopedia" by Shusuke Yui. * "Shrines of Itoshima" by the Itoshima Shinto Priest Association. {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:細石神社]] == References == * ''Itoshima Shima Chiri Zenshi'' by Yui Shōyū * ''Shrine of the Guardian Deity'' by the Itoshima Shinto Clergy Association {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:細石神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:糸島市の歴史]] {{Commons category|Sazareishi-jinja}}

木之花開耶姫命 | 社格=村社 | 例祭=10月25日 例大祭 }} '''Sazareishi Jinja''' (細石神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. Historically referred to as "Sasareiishi Jinja" (佐々禮石神社), the enshrined deities are the sister goddesses {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|磐長姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワナガヒメ}} and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. It was classified as a {{ill|Village shrine (Shinto)|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Shinto)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Overview == The shrine is presumed to be situated in the central area of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]]. According to the shrine history titled "Sazareishi Jinja Goengi," compiled in the [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] era (1695), the shrine was once a large and significant establishment with extensive sacred rice fields. However, repeated conflicts resulted in the destruction of the shrine's buildings. Furthermore, during the {{ill|Taikō land survey|ja|太閤検地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taikō land survey|qq=}} conducted by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] era (1587), the shrine's sacred lands were confiscated, leading to its decline. Today, a stone pillar labeled "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Jinja" stands next to the torii gate. == Access == The shrine can be reached by taking a bus bound for Iharayama from [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|JR Kyushu]]'s [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] at [[:en:Hatae Station|Hatae Station]]. Disembark at Mikumomiya (Sazareishi Jinja-mae) after approximately 10 minutes. ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name= Sazareishi Shrine | image = [[File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|300px|Sazareishi Shrine]] | image Caption = Worship Hall of Sazareishi Shrine | location=Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | Latitude = 33|Latitude_min = 32|Latitude_sec = 13.18 | Longitude =130|Longitude_min = 14|Longitude_sec = 33.04 | Map=Japan Fukuoka | deity=Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto
Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto | Shrine Rank=Village Shrine | Annual Festival=October 25 }} '''Sazareishi Shrine''' (細石神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. Historically, it was written as "'''Sasareishi Shrine'''" (佐々禮石神社). The enshrined deities are the sister goddesses [[Iwanaga-hime]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|former shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a village shrine. == Overview == The shrine is believed to be located in the central area of the ancient [[Itokoku]] region. According to the "Sazareishi Shrine Origin Record" from the 8th year of [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] (1695), the shrine once had many sacred rice fields and was a large shrine. However, it suffered from repeated military conflicts, leading to the destruction of its shrine buildings. Furthermore, during the [[Taikō land survey]] conducted by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 15th year of [[Tenshō]] (1587), the shrine's sacred rice fields were confiscated, causing its decline. Currently, a stone pillar inscribed with "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Shrine" stands beside the torii gate. == Access == Approximately 10 minutes by bus towards Iharayama from [[:en:Hatae Station|Hatae Station]] on the [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] operated by [[JR Kyushu]]. Get off at Mikumo Shrine (Sazareishi Shrine) bus stop. == References == * ''Itoshima and Ido Geography Complete Encyclopedia'' by Shoyu Yui * ''Shrines of Guardian Deities'' by Itoshima Shinto Priest Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:%E7%B4%B0%E7%9F%B3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] == References == * "Itoshima Geographical Encyclopedia" by Shusuke Yui. * "Shrines of Itoshima" by the Itoshima Shinto Priest Association. {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:細石神社]] == References == * ''Itoshima Shima Chiri Zenshi'' by Yui Shōyū * ''Shrine of the Guardian Deity'' by the Itoshima Shinto Clergy Association {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:細石神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:糸島市の歴史]] {{Commons category|Sazareishi-jinja}}
