According to the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Former Shrine Rank]], its status is Village Shrine. == Overview == It is presumed to be located in the central part of [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]]. == Access == It is about a 10‑minute bus ride bound for Iharayama from [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] [[:en:Hatae Station|Hatae Station]], alighting at Mikumo Shrine (Sazareishi Jinja) bus stop. ==Overview== {{infobox religious building | name = Sazareishi Shrine|native_name= 細石神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|300px|細石神社]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|imageキャプション = 細石神社拝殿 | address=福岡県糸島市三雲432 | 緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 32|緯度秒 = 13.18 | 経度度 =130|経度分 = 14|経度秒 = 33.04 | 地図=Japan Fukuoka | 祭神=磐長姫命
木之花開耶姫命 | 社格=村社 | 例祭=10月25日 例大祭 }} '''Sazareishi Jinja''' (細石神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. Historically referred to as "Sasareiishi Jinja" (佐々禮石神社), the enshrined deities are the sister goddesses {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|磐長姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワナガヒメ}} and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. It was classified as a {{ill|Village shrine (Shinto)|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Shinto)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Overview == The shrine is presumed to be situated in the central area of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]]. According to the shrine history titled "Sazareishi Jinja Goengi," compiled in the [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] era (1695), the shrine was once a large and significant establishment with extensive sacred rice fields. However, repeated conflicts resulted in the destruction of the shrine's buildings. Furthermore, during the {{ill|Taikō land survey|ja|太閤検地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taikō land survey|qq=}} conducted by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] era (1587), the shrine's sacred lands were confiscated, leading to its decline. Today, a stone pillar labeled "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Jinja" stands next to the torii gate. == Access == The shrine can be reached by taking a bus bound for Iharayama from [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|JR Kyushu]]'s [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] at [[:en:Hatae Station|Hatae Station]]. Disembark at Mikumomiya (Sazareishi Jinja-mae) after approximately 10 minutes. ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name= Sazareishi Shrine | image = [[File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|300px|Sazareishi Shrine]] | image Caption = Worship Hall of Sazareishi Shrine | location=Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | Latitude = 33|Latitude_min = 32|Latitude_sec = 13.18 | Longitude =130|Longitude_min = 14|Longitude_sec = 33.04 | Map=Japan Fukuoka | deity=Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto
Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto | Shrine Rank=Village Shrine | Annual Festival=October 25 }} '''Sazareishi Shrine''' (細石神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. Historically, it was written as "'''Sasareishi Shrine'''" (佐々禮石神社). The enshrined deities are the sister goddesses [[Iwanaga-hime]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime]]. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|former shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a village shrine. == Overview == The shrine is believed to be located in the central area of the ancient [[Itokoku]] region. According to the "Sazareishi Shrine Origin Record" from the 8th year of [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] (1695), the shrine once had many sacred rice fields and was a large shrine. However, it suffered from repeated military conflicts, leading to the destruction of its shrine buildings. Furthermore, during the [[Taikō land survey]] conducted by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 15th year of [[Tenshō]] (1587), the shrine's sacred rice fields were confiscated, causing its decline. Currently, a stone pillar inscribed with "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Shrine" stands beside the torii gate. == Access == Approximately 10 minutes by bus towards Iharayama from [[:en:Hatae Station|Hatae Station]] on the [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] operated by [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]]. Get off at Mikumo Shrine (Sazareishi Shrine) bus stop. == References == * ''Itoshima and Ido Geography Complete Encyclopedia'' by Shoyu Yui * ''Shrines of Guardian Deities'' by Itoshima Shinto Priest Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:%E7%B4%B0%E7%9F%B3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] == References == * "Itoshima Geographical Encyclopedia" by Shusuke Yui. * "Shrines of Itoshima" by the Itoshima Shinto Priest Association. {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:細石神社]] == References == * ''Itoshima Shima Chiri Zenshi'' by Yui Shōyū * ''Shrine of the Guardian Deity'' by the Itoshima Shinto Clergy Association {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:細石神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] {{Commons category|Sazareishi-jinja}}

According to the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Former Shrine Rank]], its status is Village Shrine. == Overview == It is presumed to be located in the central part of [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]]. == Access == It is about a 10‑minute bus ride bound for Iharayama from [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] [[:en:Hatae Station|Hatae Station]], alighting at Mikumo Shrine (Sazareishi Jinja) bus stop. ==Overview== {{infobox religious building | name = Sazareishi Shrine|native_name= 細石神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|300px|細石神社]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|imageキャプション = 細石神社拝殿 | address=福岡県糸島市三雲432 | 緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 32|緯度秒 = 13.18 | 経度度 =130|経度分 = 14|経度秒 = 33.04 | 地図=Japan Fukuoka | 祭神=磐長姫命
木之花開耶姫命 | 社格=村社 | 例祭=10月25日 例大祭 }} '''Sazareishi Jinja''' (細石神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. Historically referred to as "Sasareiishi Jinja" (佐々禮石神社), the enshrined deities are the sister goddesses {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|磐長姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワナガヒメ}} and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. It was classified as a {{ill|Village shrine (Shinto)|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Shinto)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Overview == The shrine is presumed to be situated in the central area of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]]. According to the shrine history titled "Sazareishi Jinja Goengi," compiled in the [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] era (1695), the shrine was once a large and significant establishment with extensive sacred rice fields. However, repeated conflicts resulted in the destruction of the shrine's buildings. Furthermore, during the {{ill|Taikō land survey|ja|太閤検地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taikō land survey|qq=}} conducted by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] era (1587), the shrine's sacred lands were confiscated, leading to its decline. Today, a stone pillar labeled "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Jinja" stands next to the torii gate. == Access == The shrine can be reached by taking a bus bound for Iharayama from [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|JR Kyushu]]'s [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] at [[:en:Hatae Station|Hatae Station]]. Disembark at Mikumomiya (Sazareishi Jinja-mae) after approximately 10 minutes. ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name= Sazareishi Shrine | image = [[File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|300px|Sazareishi Shrine]] | image Caption = Worship Hall of Sazareishi Shrine | location=Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | Latitude = 33|Latitude_min = 32|Latitude_sec = 13.18 | Longitude =130|Longitude_min = 14|Longitude_sec = 33.04 | Map=Japan Fukuoka | deity=Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto
Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto | Shrine Rank=Village Shrine | Annual Festival=October 25 }} '''Sazareishi Shrine''' (細石神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. Historically, it was written as "'''Sasareishi Shrine'''" (佐々禮石神社). The enshrined deities are the sister goddesses [[Iwanaga-hime]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime]]. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|former shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a village shrine. == Overview == The shrine is believed to be located in the central area of the ancient [[Itokoku]] region. According to the "Sazareishi Shrine Origin Record" from the 8th year of [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] (1695), the shrine once had many sacred rice fields and was a large shrine. However, it suffered from repeated military conflicts, leading to the destruction of its shrine buildings. Furthermore, during the [[Taikō land survey]] conducted by [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 15th year of [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] (1587), the shrine's sacred rice fields were confiscated, causing its decline. Currently, a stone pillar inscribed with "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Shrine" stands beside the torii gate. == Access == Approximately 10 minutes by bus towards Iharayama from [[:en:Hatae Station|Hatae Station]] on the [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] operated by [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]]. Get off at Mikumo Shrine (Sazareishi Shrine) bus stop. == References == * ''Itoshima and Ido Geography Complete Encyclopedia'' by Shoyu Yui * ''Shrines of Guardian Deities'' by Itoshima Shinto Priest Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:%E7%B4%B0%E7%9F%B3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] == References == * "Itoshima Geographical Encyclopedia" by Shusuke Yui. * "Shrines of Itoshima" by the Itoshima Shinto Priest Association. {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:細石神社]] == References == * ''Itoshima Shima Chiri Zenshi'' by Yui Shōyū * ''Shrine of the Guardian Deity'' by the Itoshima Shinto Clergy Association {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:細石神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] {{Commons category|Sazareishi-jinja}}

In ancient times, it was written as "'''Sasareishi Shrine'''". The enshrined deities are the two sister kami, [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanagahime]] and [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohananosakuyahime]]. According to the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Former Shrine Rank]], its status is Village Shrine. == Overview == It is presumed to be located in the central part of [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]]. == Access == It is about a 10‑minute bus ride bound for Iharayama from [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] [[:en:Hatae Station|Hatae Station]], alighting at Mikumo Shrine (Sazareishi Jinja) bus stop. ==Overview== {{infobox religious building | name = Sazareishi Shrine|native_name= 細石神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|300px|細石神社]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|imageキャプション = 細石神社拝殿 | address=福岡県糸島市三雲432 | 緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 32|緯度秒 = 13.18 | 経度度 =130|経度分 = 14|経度秒 = 33.04 | 地図=Japan Fukuoka | 祭神=磐長姫命
木之花開耶姫命 | 社格=村社 | 例祭=10月25日 例大祭 }} '''Sazareishi Jinja''' (細石神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. Historically referred to as "Sasareiishi Jinja" (佐々禮石神社), the enshrined deities are the sister goddesses {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|磐長姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワナガヒメ}} and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. It was classified as a {{ill|Village shrine (Shinto)|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Shinto)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Overview == The shrine is presumed to be situated in the central area of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]]. According to the shrine history titled "Sazareishi Jinja Goengi," compiled in the [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] era (1695), the shrine was once a large and significant establishment with extensive sacred rice fields. However, repeated conflicts resulted in the destruction of the shrine's buildings. Furthermore, during the {{ill|Taikō land survey|ja|太閤検地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taikō land survey|qq=}} conducted by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] era (1587), the shrine's sacred lands were confiscated, leading to its decline. Today, a stone pillar labeled "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Jinja" stands next to the torii gate. == Access == The shrine can be reached by taking a bus bound for Iharayama from [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|JR Kyushu]]'s [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] at [[:en:Hatae Station|Hatae Station]]. Disembark at Mikumomiya (Sazareishi Jinja-mae) after approximately 10 minutes. ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name= Sazareishi Shrine | image = [[File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|300px|Sazareishi Shrine]] | image Caption = Worship Hall of Sazareishi Shrine | location=Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | Latitude = 33|Latitude_min = 32|Latitude_sec = 13.18 | Longitude =130|Longitude_min = 14|Longitude_sec = 33.04 | Map=Japan Fukuoka | deity=Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto
Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto | Shrine Rank=Village Shrine | Annual Festival=October 25 }} '''Sazareishi Shrine''' (細石神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. Historically, it was written as "'''Sasareishi Shrine'''" (佐々禮石神社). The enshrined deities are the sister goddesses [[Iwanaga-hime]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime]]. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|former shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a village shrine. == Overview == The shrine is believed to be located in the central area of the ancient [[Itokoku]] region. According to the "Sazareishi Shrine Origin Record" from the 8th year of [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] (1695), the shrine once had many sacred rice fields and was a large shrine. However, it suffered from repeated military conflicts, leading to the destruction of its shrine buildings. Furthermore, during the [[Taikō land survey]] conducted by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 15th year of [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] (1587), the shrine's sacred rice fields were confiscated, causing its decline. Currently, a stone pillar inscribed with "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Shrine" stands beside the torii gate. == Access == Approximately 10 minutes by bus towards Iharayama from [[:en:Hatae Station|Hatae Station]] on the [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] operated by [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]]. Get off at Mikumo Shrine (Sazareishi Shrine) bus stop. == References == * ''Itoshima and Ido Geography Complete Encyclopedia'' by Shoyu Yui * ''Shrines of Guardian Deities'' by Itoshima Shinto Priest Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[sn:E7%B4%B0%E7%9F%B3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] == References == * "Itoshima Geographical Encyclopedia" by Shusuke Yui. * "Shrines of Itoshima" by the Itoshima Shinto Priest Association. {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:細石神社]] == References == * ''Itoshima Shima Chiri Zenshi'' by Yui Shōyū * ''Shrine of the Guardian Deity'' by the Itoshima Shinto Clergy Association {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:細石神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] {{Commons category|Sazareishi-jinja}} [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11606084]] Wikidata name: Sazareishi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:26, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Shrine | Name = Sazareishi Jinja (細石神社) | image = {{ill|File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|ja|Sazareishi Jinja|lt=300px|lt_ja=細石神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Image Caption = Sazareishi Shrine Haiden (細石神社拝殿) | Location = 432, Mikumo, Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture | Latitude Degrees = 33 | Latitude Minutes = 32 | Latitude Seconds = 13.18 | Longitude Degrees = 130 | Longitude Minutes = 14 | Longitude Seconds = 33.04 | Map = Japan Fukuoka | Deities = Iwanagahime-no-mikoto (磐長姫命)
Konohananosakuyahime-no-mikoto (木之花開耶姫命) | Status = Village Shrine | Annual Festival = October 25, Annual Grand Festival }} '''Sazareishi Jinja''' (細石神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]]. In ancient times, it was written as "'''Sasareishi Shrine'''". The enshrined deities are the two sister kami, [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanagahime]] and [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohananosakuyahime]]. According to the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Former Shrine Rank]], its status is Village Shrine. == Overview == It is presumed to be located in the central part of [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]]. == Access == It is about a 10‑minute bus ride bound for Iharayama from [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] [[:en:Hatae Station|Hatae Station]], alighting at Mikumo Shrine (Sazareishi Jinja) bus stop. ==Overview== {{infobox religious building | name = Sazareishi Shrine|native_name= 細石神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|300px|細石神社]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|imageキャプション = 細石神社拝殿 | address=福岡県糸島市三雲432 | 緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 32|緯度秒 = 13.18 | 経度度 =130|経度分 = 14|経度秒 = 33.04 | 地図=Japan Fukuoka | 祭神=磐長姫命
木之花開耶姫命 | 社格=村社 | 例祭=10月25日 例大祭 }} '''Sazareishi Jinja''' (細石神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. Historically referred to as "Sasareiishi Jinja" (佐々禮石神社), the enshrined deities are the sister goddesses {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|磐長姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワナガヒメ}} and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. It was classified as a {{ill|Village shrine (Shinto)|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Shinto)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Overview == The shrine is presumed to be situated in the central area of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]]. According to the shrine history titled "Sazareishi Jinja Goengi," compiled in the [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] era (1695), the shrine was once a large and significant establishment with extensive sacred rice fields. However, repeated conflicts resulted in the destruction of the shrine's buildings. Furthermore, during the {{ill|Taikō land survey|ja|太閤検地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taikō land survey|qq=}} conducted by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] era (1587), the shrine's sacred lands were confiscated, leading to its decline. Today, a stone pillar labeled "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Jinja" stands next to the torii gate. == Access == The shrine can be reached by taking a bus bound for Iharayama from [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|JR Kyushu]]'s [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] at [[:en:Hatae Station|Hatae Station]]. Disembark at Mikumomiya (Sazareishi Jinja-mae) after approximately 10 minutes. ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name= Sazareishi Shrine | image = [[File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|300px|Sazareishi Shrine]] | image Caption = Worship Hall of Sazareishi Shrine | location=Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | Latitude = 33|Latitude_min = 32|Latitude_sec = 13.18 | Longitude =130|Longitude_min = 14|Longitude_sec = 33.04 | Map=Japan Fukuoka | deity=Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto
Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto | Shrine Rank=Village Shrine | Annual Festival=October 25 }} '''Sazareishi Shrine''' (細石神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. Historically, it was written as "'''Sasareishi Shrine'''" (佐々禮石神社). The enshrined deities are the sister goddesses [[Iwanaga-hime]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime]]. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|former shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a village shrine. == Overview == The shrine is believed to be located in the central area of the ancient [[Itokoku]] region. According to the "Sazareishi Shrine Origin Record" from the 8th year of [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] (1695), the shrine once had many sacred rice fields and was a large shrine. However, it suffered from repeated military conflicts, leading to the destruction of its shrine buildings. Furthermore, during the [[Taikō land survey]] conducted by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 15th year of [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] (1587), the shrine's sacred rice fields were confiscated, causing its decline. Currently, a stone pillar inscribed with "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Shrine" stands beside the torii gate. == Access == Approximately 10 minutes by bus towards Iharayama from [[:en:Hatae Station|Hatae Station]] on the [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] operated by [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]]. Get off at Mikumo Shrine (Sazareishi Shrine) bus stop. == References == * ''Itoshima and Ido Geography Complete Encyclopedia'' by Shoyu Yui * ''Shrines of Guardian Deities'' by Itoshima Shinto Priest Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[sn:E7%B4%B0%E7%9F%B3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] == References == * "Itoshima Geographical Encyclopedia" by Shusuke Yui. * "Shrines of Itoshima" by the Itoshima Shinto Priest Association. {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:細石神社]] == References == * ''Itoshima Shima Chiri Zenshi'' by Yui Shōyū * ''Shrine of the Guardian Deity'' by the Itoshima Shinto Clergy Association {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:細石神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] {{Commons category|Sazareishi-jinja}}

Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|former shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a village shrine. == Overview == The shrine is believed to be located in the central area of the ancient [[Itokoku]] region. According to the "Sazareishi Shrine Origin Record" from the 8th year of [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] (1695), the shrine once had many sacred rice fields and was a large shrine. However, it suffered from repeated military conflicts, leading to the destruction of its shrine buildings. Furthermore, during the [[Taikō land survey]] conducted by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 15th year of [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] (1587), the shrine's sacred rice fields were confiscated, causing its decline. Currently, a stone pillar inscribed with "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Shrine" stands beside the torii gate. == Access == Approximately 10 minutes by bus towards Iharayama from [[:en:Hatae Station|Hatae Station]] on the [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] operated by [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]]. Get off at Mikumo Shrine (Sazareishi Shrine) bus stop. == References == * ''Itoshima and Ido Geography Complete Encyclopedia'' by Shoyu Yui * ''Shrines of Guardian Deities'' by Itoshima Shinto Priest Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[sn:E7%B4%B0%E7%9F%B3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] == References == * "Itoshima Geographical Encyclopedia" by Shusuke Yui. * "Shrines of Itoshima" by the Itoshima Shinto Priest Association. {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:細石神社]] == References == * ''Itoshima Shima Chiri Zenshi'' by Yui Shōyū * ''Shrine of the Guardian Deity'' by the Itoshima Shinto Clergy Association {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:細石神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] {{Commons category|Sazareishi-jinja}}

Konohananosakuyahime-no-mikoto (木之花開耶姫命) | Status = Village Shrine | Annual Festival = October 25, Annual Grand Festival | location = {{ill|Itoshima|qid=Q1011145}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33.5369944|N|130.2425111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Sazareishi Jinja''' (細石神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]]. In ancient times, it was written as "'''Sasareishi Shrine'''". The enshrined deities are the two sister kami, [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanagahime]] and [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohananosakuyahime]]. According to the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Former Shrine Rank]], its status is Village Shrine. == Overview == It is presumed to be located in the central part of [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]]. == Access == It is about a 10‑minute bus ride bound for Iharayama from [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] [[:en:Hatae Station|Hatae Station]], alighting at Mikumo Shrine (Sazareishi Jinja) bus stop. ==Overview== {{infobox religious building | name = Sazareishi Shrine|native_name= 細石神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|300px|細石神社]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|imageキャプション = 細石神社拝殿 | address=福岡県糸島市三雲432 | 緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 32|緯度秒 = 13.18 | 経度度 =130|経度分 = 14|経度秒 = 33.04 | 地図=Japan Fukuoka | 祭神=磐長姫命
木之花開耶姫命 | 社格=村社 | 例祭=10月25日 例大祭 }} '''Sazareishi Jinja''' (細石神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. Historically referred to as "Sasareiishi Jinja" (佐々禮石神社), the enshrined deities are the sister goddesses {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|磐長姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワナガヒメ}} and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. It was classified as a {{ill|Village shrine (Shinto)|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Shinto)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Overview == The shrine is presumed to be situated in the central area of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]]. According to the shrine history titled "Sazareishi Jinja Goengi," compiled in the [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] era (1695), the shrine was once a large and significant establishment with extensive sacred rice fields. However, repeated conflicts resulted in the destruction of the shrine's buildings. Furthermore, during the {{ill|Taikō land survey|ja|太閤検地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taikō land survey|qq=}} conducted by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] era (1587), the shrine's sacred lands were confiscated, leading to its decline. Today, a stone pillar labeled "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Jinja" stands next to the torii gate. == Access == The shrine can be reached by taking a bus bound for Iharayama from [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|JR Kyushu]]'s [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] at [[:en:Hatae Station|Hatae Station]]. Disembark at Mikumomiya (Sazareishi Jinja-mae) after approximately 10 minutes. ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name= Sazareishi Shrine | image = [[File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|300px|Sazareishi Shrine]] | image Caption = Worship Hall of Sazareishi Shrine | location=Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | Latitude = 33|Latitude_min = 32|Latitude_sec = 13.18 | Longitude =130|Longitude_min = 14|Longitude_sec = 33.04 | Map=Japan Fukuoka | deity=Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto
Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto | Shrine Rank=Village Shrine | Annual Festival=October 25 }} '''Sazareishi Shrine''' (細石神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. Historically, it was written as "'''Sasareishi Shrine'''" (佐々禮石神社). The enshrined deities are the sister goddesses [[Iwanaga-hime]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime]]. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|former shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a village shrine. == Overview == The shrine is believed to be located in the central area of the ancient [[Itokoku]] region. According to the "Sazareishi Shrine Origin Record" from the 8th year of [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] (1695), the shrine once had many sacred rice fields and was a large shrine. However, it suffered from repeated military conflicts, leading to the destruction of its shrine buildings. Furthermore, during the [[Taikō land survey]] conducted by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 15th year of [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] (1587), the shrine's sacred rice fields were confiscated, causing its decline. Currently, a stone pillar inscribed with "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Shrine" stands beside the torii gate. == Access == Approximately 10 minutes by bus towards Iharayama from [[:en:Hatae Station|Hatae Station]] on the [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] operated by [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]]. Get off at Mikumo Shrine (Sazareishi Shrine) bus stop. == References == * ''Itoshima and Ido Geography Complete Encyclopedia'' by Shoyu Yui * ''Shrines of Guardian Deities'' by Itoshima Shinto Priest Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[sn:E7%B4%B0%E7%9F%B3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] == References == * "Itoshima Geographical Encyclopedia" by Shusuke Yui. * "Shrines of Itoshima" by the Itoshima Shinto Priest Association. {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:細石神社]] == References == * ''Itoshima Shima Chiri Zenshi'' by Yui Shōyū * ''Shrine of the Guardian Deity'' by the Itoshima Shinto Clergy Association {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:細石神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] {{Commons category|Sazareishi-jinja}}

Disembark at Mikumomiya (Sazareishi Jinja-mae) after approximately 10 minutes. ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name= Sazareishi Shrine | image = [[File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|300px|Sazareishi Shrine]] | image Caption = Worship Hall of Sazareishi Shrine | location=Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | Latitude = 33|Latitude_min = 32|Latitude_sec = 13.18 | Longitude =130|Longitude_min = 14|Longitude_sec = 33.04 | Map=Japan Fukuoka | deity=Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto
Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto | Shrine Rank=Village Shrine | Annual Festival=October 25 }} '''Sazareishi Shrine''' (細石神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. Historically, it was written as "'''Sasareishi Shrine'''" (佐々禮石神社). The enshrined deities are the sister goddesses [[Iwanaga-hime]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime]]. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|former shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a village shrine. == Overview == The shrine is believed to be located in the central area of the ancient [[Itokoku]] region. According to the "Sazareishi Shrine Origin Record" from the 8th year of [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] (1695), the shrine once had many sacred rice fields and was a large shrine. However, it suffered from repeated military conflicts, leading to the destruction of its shrine buildings. Furthermore, during the [[Taikō land survey]] conducted by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 15th year of [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] (1587), the shrine's sacred rice fields were confiscated, causing its decline. Currently, a stone pillar inscribed with "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Shrine" stands beside the torii gate. == Access == Approximately 10 minutes by bus towards Iharayama from [[:en:Hatae Station|Hatae Station]] on the [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] operated by [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]]. Get off at Mikumo Shrine (Sazareishi Shrine) bus stop. == References == * ''Itoshima and Ido Geography Complete Encyclopedia'' by Shoyu Yui * ''Shrines of Guardian Deities'' by Itoshima Shinto Priest Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[sn:E7%B4%B0%E7%9F%B3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] == References == * "Itoshima Geographical Encyclopedia" by Shusuke Yui. * "Shrines of Itoshima" by the Itoshima Shinto Priest Association. {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:細石神社]] == References == * ''Itoshima Shima Chiri Zenshi'' by Yui Shōyū * ''Shrine of the Guardian Deity'' by the Itoshima Shinto Clergy Association {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:細石神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Sazareishi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages to redo the thing on]] {{Commons category|Sazareishi-jinja}}

Historically referred to as "Sasareiishi Jinja" (佐々禮石神社), the enshrined deities are the sister goddesses {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|磐長姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワナガヒメ}} and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. It was classified as a {{ill|Village shrine (Shinto)|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Shinto)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Overview == The shrine is presumed to be situated in the central area of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]]. According to the shrine history titled "Sazareishi Jinja Goengi," compiled in the [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] era (1695), the shrine was once a large and significant establishment with extensive sacred rice fields. Historically referred to as "Sasareiishi Jinja" (佐々禮石神社), the enshrined deities are the sister goddesses {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|磐長姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワナガヒメ}} and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. It was classified as a {{ill|Village shrine (Shinto)|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Shinto)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Overview == The shrine is presumed to be situated in the central area of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]]. According to the shrine history titled "Sazareishi Jinja Goengi," compiled in the [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] era (1695), the shrine was once a large and significant establishment with extensive sacred rice fields. However, repeated conflicts resulted in the destruction of the shrine's buildings. Furthermore, during the {{ill|Taikō land survey|ja|太閤検地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taikō land survey|qq=|1=Taikō kenchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193888}} conducted by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] era (1587), the shrine's sacred lands were confiscated, leading to its decline. Today, a stone pillar labeled "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Jinja" stands next to the torii gate. == Access == The shrine can be reached by taking a bus bound for Iharayama from [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|JR Kyushu]]'s [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] at [[:en:Hatae Station|Hatae Station]]. Disembark at Mikumomiya (Sazareishi Jinja-mae) after approximately 10 minutes. ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name= Sazareishi Shrine | image = [[File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|300px|Sazareishi Shrine]] | image Caption = Worship Hall of Sazareishi Shrine | location=Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | Latitude = 33|Latitude_min = 32|Latitude_sec = 13.18 | Longitude =130|Longitude_min = 14|Longitude_sec = 33.04 | Map=Japan Fukuoka | deity=Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto
Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto | Shrine Rank=Village Shrine | Annual Festival=October 25 }} '''Sazareishi Shrine''' (細石神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. Historically, it was written as "'''Sasareishi Shrine'''" (佐々禮石神社). The enshrined deities are the sister goddesses [[Iwanaga-hime]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime]]. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|former shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a village shrine. == Overview == The shrine is believed to be located in the central area of the ancient [[Itokoku]] region. According to the "Sazareishi Shrine Origin Record" from the 8th year of [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] (1695), the shrine once had many sacred rice fields and was a large shrine. However, it suffered from repeated military conflicts, leading to the destruction of its shrine buildings. Furthermore, during the [[Taikō land survey]] conducted by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 15th year of [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] (1587), the shrine's sacred rice fields were confiscated, causing its decline. Currently, a stone pillar inscribed with "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Shrine" stands beside the torii gate. == Access == Approximately 10 minutes by bus towards Iharayama from [[:en:Hatae Station|Hatae Station]] on the [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] operated by [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]]. Get off at Mikumo Shrine (Sazareishi Shrine) bus stop. == References == * ''Itoshima and Ido Geography Complete Encyclopedia'' by Shoyu Yui * ''Shrines of Guardian Deities'' by Itoshima Shinto Priest Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[sn:E7%B4%B0%E7%9F%B3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] == References == * "Itoshima Geographical Encyclopedia" by Shusuke Yui. * "Shrines of Itoshima" by the Itoshima Shinto Priest Association. {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:細石神社]] == References == * ''Itoshima Shima Chiri Zenshi'' by Yui Shōyū * ''Shrine of the Guardian Deity'' by the Itoshima Shinto Clergy Association {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] }} [[sn:Sazareishi Shrine]] [[ja:細石神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] [[en:Sazareishi Shrine]][[Category:pages to redo the thing on]] {{Commons category|Sazareishi-jinja}}

revision.comment: Bot: remove incorrect Shona (sn:) interwiki links
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11606084]] Wikidata name: Sazareishi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:26, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Sazareishi Jinja (細石神社) | image = File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg | Image Caption = Sazareishi Shrine Haiden (細石神社拝殿) | Location = 432, Mikumo, Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture | Latitude Degrees = 33 | Latitude Minutes = 32 | Latitude Seconds = 13.18 | Longitude Degrees = 130 | Longitude Minutes = 14 | Longitude Seconds = 33.04 | Map = Japan Fukuoka | Deities = Iwanagahime-no-mikoto (磐長姫命)
Konohananosakuyahime-no-mikoto (木之花開耶姫命) | Status = Village Shrine | Annual Festival = October 25, Annual Grand Festival | location = {{ill|Itoshima|qid=Q1011145}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33.5369944|N|130.2425111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Sazareishi Jinja''' (細石神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]]. In ancient times, it was written as "'''Sasareishi Shrine'''". The enshrined deities are the two sister kami, [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanagahime]] and [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohananosakuyahime]]. According to the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Former Shrine Rank]], its status is Village Shrine. == Overview == It is presumed to be located in the central part of [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]]. == Access == It is about a 10‑minute bus ride bound for Iharayama from [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] [[:en:Hatae Station|Hatae Station]], alighting at Mikumo Shrine (Sazareishi Jinja) bus stop. ==Overview== {{infobox religious building | name = Sazareishi Shrine|native_name= 細石神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|300px|細石神社]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|imageキャプション = 細石神社拝殿 | address=福岡県糸島市三雲432 | 緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 32|緯度秒 = 13.18 | 経度度 =130|経度分 = 14|経度秒 = 33.04 | 地図=Japan Fukuoka | 祭神=磐長姫命
木之花開耶姫命 | 社格=村社 | 例祭=10月25日 例大祭 }} '''Sazareishi Jinja''' (細石神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. Historically referred to as "Sasareiishi Jinja" (佐々禮石神社), the enshrined deities are the sister goddesses {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|磐長姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワナガヒメ}} and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. It was classified as a {{ill|Village shrine (Shinto)|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Shinto)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Overview == The shrine is presumed to be situated in the central area of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]]. According to the shrine history titled "Sazareishi Jinja Goengi," compiled in the [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] era (1695), the shrine was once a large and significant establishment with extensive sacred rice fields. However, repeated conflicts resulted in the destruction of the shrine's buildings. Furthermore, during the {{ill|Taikō land survey|ja|太閤検地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taikō land survey|qq=|1=Taikō kenchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193888}} conducted by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] era (1587), the shrine's sacred lands were confiscated, leading to its decline. Today, a stone pillar labeled "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Jinja" stands next to the torii gate. == Access == The shrine can be reached by taking a bus bound for Iharayama from [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|JR Kyushu]]'s [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] at [[:en:Hatae Station|Hatae Station]]. Disembark at Mikumomiya (Sazareishi Jinja-mae) after approximately 10 minutes. ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name= Sazareishi Shrine | image = [[File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|300px|Sazareishi Shrine]] | image Caption = Worship Hall of Sazareishi Shrine | location=Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | Latitude = 33|Latitude_min = 32|Latitude_sec = 13.18 | Longitude =130|Longitude_min = 14|Longitude_sec = 33.04 | Map=Japan Fukuoka | deity=Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto
Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto | Shrine Rank=Village Shrine | Annual Festival=October 25 }} '''Sazareishi Shrine''' (細石神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. Historically, it was written as "'''Sasareishi Shrine'''" (佐々禮石神社). The enshrined deities are the sister goddesses [[Iwanaga-hime]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime]]. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|former shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a village shrine. == Overview == The shrine is believed to be located in the central area of the ancient [[Itokoku]] region. According to the "Sazareishi Shrine Origin Record" from the 8th year of [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] (1695), the shrine once had many sacred rice fields and was a large shrine. However, it suffered from repeated military conflicts, leading to the destruction of its shrine buildings. Furthermore, during the [[Taikō land survey]] conducted by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 15th year of [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] (1587), the shrine's sacred rice fields were confiscated, causing its decline. Currently, a stone pillar inscribed with "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Shrine" stands beside the torii gate. == Access == Approximately 10 minutes by bus towards Iharayama from [[:en:Hatae Station|Hatae Station]] on the [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] operated by [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]]. Get off at Mikumo Shrine (Sazareishi Shrine) bus stop. == References == * ''Itoshima and Ido Geography Complete Encyclopedia'' by Shoyu Yui * ''Shrines of Guardian Deities'' by Itoshima Shinto Priest Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} == References == * "Itoshima Geographical Encyclopedia" by Shusuke Yui. * "Shrines of Itoshima" by the Itoshima Shinto Priest Association. {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:細石神社]] == References == * ''Itoshima Shima Chiri Zenshi'' by Yui Shōyū * ''Shrine of the Guardian Deity'' by the Itoshima Shinto Clergy Association {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:細石神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] [[Category:pages to redo the thing on]] {{Commons category|Sazareishi-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11606084}}

Get off at Mikumo Shrine (Sazareishi Shrine) bus stop. == References == * ''Itoshima and Ido Geography Complete Encyclopedia'' by Shoyu Yui * ''Shrines of Guardian Deities'' by Itoshima Shinto Priest Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} == References == * "Itoshima Geographical Encyclopedia" by Shusuke Yui. * "Shrines of Itoshima" by the Itoshima Shinto Priest Association. {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:細石神社]] == References == * ''Itoshima Shima Chiri Zenshi'' by Yui Shōyū * ''Shrine of the Guardian Deity'' by the Itoshima Shinto Clergy Association {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:細石神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] [[Category:pages to redo the thing on]] {{Commons category|Sazareishi-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11606084}}

revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Sazareishi-jinja]][[ja:細石神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11606084]] Wikidata name: Sazareishi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:26, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = Sazareishi Jinja (細石神社) | image = File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg | Image Caption = Sazareishi Shrine Haiden (細石神社拝殿) | Location = 432, Mikumo, Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture | Latitude Degrees = 33 | Latitude Minutes = 32 | Latitude Seconds = 13.18 | Longitude Degrees = 130 | Longitude Minutes = 14 | Longitude Seconds = 33.04 | Map = Japan Fukuoka | Deities = Iwanagahime-no-mikoto (磐長姫命)
Konohananosakuyahime-no-mikoto (木之花開耶姫命) | Status = Village Shrine | Annual Festival = October 25, Annual Grand Festival | location = {{ill|Itoshima|qid=Q1011145}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33.5369944|N|130.2425111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Sazareishi Jinja''' (細石神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]]. In ancient times, it was written as "'''Sasareishi Shrine'''". The enshrined deities are the two sister kami, [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanagahime]] and [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohananosakuyahime]]. According to the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Former Shrine Rank]], its status is Village Shrine. == Overview == It is presumed to be located in the central part of [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]]. == Access == It is about a 10‑minute bus ride bound for Iharayama from [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] [[:en:Hatae Station|Hatae Station]], alighting at Mikumo Shrine (Sazareishi Jinja) bus stop. ==Overview== {{infobox religious building | name = Sazareishi Shrine|native_name= 細石神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|300px|細石神社]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|imageキャプション = 細石神社拝殿 | address=福岡県糸島市三雲432 | 緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 32|緯度秒 = 13.18 | 経度度 =130|経度分 = 14|経度秒 = 33.04 | 地図=Japan Fukuoka | 祭神=磐長姫命
木之花開耶姫命 | 社格=村社 | 例祭=10月25日 例大祭 }} '''Sazareishi Jinja''' (細石神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. Historically referred to as "Sasareiishi Jinja" (佐々禮石神社), the enshrined deities are the sister goddesses {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|磐長姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワナガヒメ}} and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. It was classified as a {{ill|Village shrine (Shinto)|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Shinto)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Overview == The shrine is presumed to be situated in the central area of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]]. According to the shrine history titled "Sazareishi Jinja Goengi," compiled in the [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] era (1695), the shrine was once a large and significant establishment with extensive sacred rice fields. However, repeated conflicts resulted in the destruction of the shrine's buildings. Furthermore, during the {{ill|Taikō land survey|ja|太閤検地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taikō land survey|qq=|1=Taikō kenchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193888}} conducted by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] era (1587), the shrine's sacred lands were confiscated, leading to its decline. Today, a stone pillar labeled "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Jinja" stands next to the torii gate. == Access == The shrine can be reached by taking a bus bound for Iharayama from [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|JR Kyushu]]'s [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] at [[:en:Hatae Station|Hatae Station]]. Disembark at Mikumomiya (Sazareishi Jinja-mae) after approximately 10 minutes. ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name= Sazareishi Shrine | image = [[File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|300px|Sazareishi Shrine]] | image Caption = Worship Hall of Sazareishi Shrine | location=Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | Latitude = 33|Latitude_min = 32|Latitude_sec = 13.18 | Longitude =130|Longitude_min = 14|Longitude_sec = 33.04 | Map=Japan Fukuoka | deity=Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto
Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto | Shrine Rank=Village Shrine | Annual Festival=October 25 }} '''Sazareishi Shrine''' (細石神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. Historically, it was written as "'''Sasareishi Shrine'''" (佐々禮石神社). The enshrined deities are the sister goddesses [[Iwanaga-hime]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime]]. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|former shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a village shrine. == Overview == The shrine is believed to be located in the central area of the ancient [[Itokoku]] region. According to the "Sazareishi Shrine Origin Record" from the 8th year of [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] (1695), the shrine once had many sacred rice fields and was a large shrine. However, it suffered from repeated military conflicts, leading to the destruction of its shrine buildings. Furthermore, during the [[Taikō land survey]] conducted by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 15th year of [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] (1587), the shrine's sacred rice fields were confiscated, causing its decline. Currently, a stone pillar inscribed with "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Shrine" stands beside the torii gate. == Access == Approximately 10 minutes by bus towards Iharayama from [[:en:Hatae Station|Hatae Station]] on the [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] operated by [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]]. Get off at Mikumo Shrine (Sazareishi Shrine) bus stop. == References == * ''Itoshima and Ido Geography Complete Encyclopedia'' by Shoyu Yui * ''Shrines of Guardian Deities'' by Itoshima Shinto Priest Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} == References == * "Itoshima Geographical Encyclopedia" by Shusuke Yui. * "Shrines of Itoshima" by the Itoshima Shinto Priest Association. {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:細石神社]] == References == * ''Itoshima Shima Chiri Zenshi'' by Yui Shōyū * ''Shrine of the Guardian Deity'' by the Itoshima Shinto Clergy Association {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:細石神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] {{Commons category|Sazareishi-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11606084}}

Historically, it was written as "'''Sasareishi Shrine'''" (佐々禮石神社). The enshrined deities are the sister goddesses [[Iwanaga-hime]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime]]. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|former shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a village shrine. == Overview == The shrine is believed to be located in the central area of the ancient [[Itokoku]] region. According to the "Sazareishi Shrine Origin Record" from the 8th year of [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] (1695), the shrine once had many sacred rice fields and was a large shrine. However, it suffered from repeated military conflicts, leading to the destruction of its shrine buildings. Furthermore, during the [[Taikō land survey]] conducted by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 15th year of [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] (1587), the shrine's sacred rice fields were confiscated, causing its decline. Currently, a stone pillar inscribed with "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Shrine" stands beside the torii gate. == Access == Approximately 10 minutes by bus towards Iharayama from [[:en:Hatae Station|Hatae Station]] on the [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] operated by [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]]. Get off at Mikumo Shrine (Sazareishi Shrine) bus stop. == References == * ''Itoshima and Ido Geography Complete Encyclopedia'' by Shoyu Yui * ''Shrines of Guardian Deities'' by Itoshima Shinto Priest Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} == References == * "Itoshima Geographical Encyclopedia" by Shusuke Yui. * "Shrines of Itoshima" by the Itoshima Shinto Priest Association. {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:細石神社]] == References == * ''Itoshima Shima Chiri Zenshi'' by Yui Shōyū * ''Shrine of the Guardian Deity'' by the Itoshima Shinto Clergy Association {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:細石神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] {{Commons category|Sazareishi-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11606084}}

Konohananosakuyahime-no-mikoto (木之花開耶姫命) | Status = Village Shrine | Annual Festival = October 25, Annual Grand Festival | location = {{ill|Itoshima|WD=Q1011145}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33.5369944|N|130.2425111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Sazareishi Jinja''' (細石神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima City]]. In ancient times, it was written as "'''Sasareishi Shrine'''". The enshrined deities are the two sister kami, [[:en:Iwanaga-hime|Iwanagahime]] and [[:en:Konohanasakuya-hime|Konohananosakuyahime]]. According to the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Former Shrine Rank]], its status is Village Shrine. == Overview == It is presumed to be located in the central part of [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]]. == Access == It is about a 10‑minute bus ride bound for Iharayama from [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] [[:en:Hatae Station|Hatae Station]], alighting at Mikumo Shrine (Sazareishi Jinja) bus stop. ==Overview== {{infobox religious building | name = Sazareishi Shrine|native_name= 細石神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = [[File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|300px|細石神社]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|imageキャプション = 細石神社拝殿 | address=福岡県糸島市三雲432 | 緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 32|緯度秒 = 13.18 | 経度度 =130|経度分 = 14|経度秒 = 33.04 | 地図=Japan Fukuoka | 祭神=磐長姫命
木之花開耶姫命 | 社格=村社 | 例祭=10月25日 例大祭 }} '''Sazareishi Jinja''' (細石神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. Historically referred to as "Sasareiishi Jinja" (佐々禮石神社), the enshrined deities are the sister goddesses {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|磐長姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワナガヒメ}} and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. It was classified as a {{ill|Village shrine (Shinto)|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Shinto)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == Overview == The shrine is presumed to be situated in the central area of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]]. According to the shrine history titled "Sazareishi Jinja Goengi," compiled in the [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] era (1695), the shrine was once a large and significant establishment with extensive sacred rice fields. However, repeated conflicts resulted in the destruction of the shrine's buildings. Furthermore, during the {{ill|Taikō land survey|ja|太閤検地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taikō land survey|1=Taikō kenchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193888}} conducted by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] era (1587), the shrine's sacred lands were confiscated, leading to its decline. Today, a stone pillar labeled "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Jinja" stands next to the torii gate. == Access == The shrine can be reached by taking a bus bound for Iharayama from [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|JR Kyushu]]'s [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] at [[:en:Hatae Station|Hatae Station]]. Disembark at Mikumomiya (Sazareishi Jinja-mae) after approximately 10 minutes. ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name= Sazareishi Shrine | image = [[File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|300px|Sazareishi Shrine]] | image Caption = Worship Hall of Sazareishi Shrine | location=Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | Latitude = 33|Latitude_min = 32|Latitude_sec = 13.18 | Longitude =130|Longitude_min = 14|Longitude_sec = 33.04 | Map=Japan Fukuoka | deity=Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto
Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto | Shrine Rank=Village Shrine | Annual Festival=October 25 }} '''Sazareishi Shrine''' (細石神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. According to the "Sazareishi Shrine Origin Record" from the 8th year of [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] (1695), the shrine once had many sacred rice fields and was a large shrine. However, it suffered from repeated military conflicts, leading to the destruction of its shrine buildings. Furthermore, during the [[Taikō land survey]] conducted by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 15th year of [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] (1587), the shrine's sacred rice fields were confiscated, causing its decline. Get off at Mikumo Shrine (Sazareishi Shrine) bus stop. == References == * ''Itoshima and Ido Geography Complete Encyclopedia'' by Shoyu Yui * ''Shrines of Guardian Deities'' by Itoshima Shinto Priest Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} == References == * "Itoshima Geographical Encyclopedia" by Shusuke Yui. * "Shrines of Itoshima" by the Itoshima Shinto Priest Association. {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:細石神社]] == References == * ''Itoshima Shima Chiri Zenshi'' by Yui Shōyū * ''Shrine of the Guardian Deity'' by the Itoshima Shinto Clergy Association {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:細石神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] {{Commons category|Sazareishi-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11606084}}

Today, a stone pillar labeled "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Jinja" stands next to the torii gate. == Access == The shrine can be reached by taking a bus bound for Iharayama from [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|JR Kyushu]]'s [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] at [[:en:Hatae Station|Hatae Station]]. Disembark at Mikumomiya (Sazareishi Jinja-mae) after approximately 10 minutes. ==Overview== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name= Sazareishi Shrine | image = [[File:Sazareishi Jinja.jpg|300px|Sazareishi Shrine]] | image Caption = Worship Hall of Sazareishi Shrine | location=Itoshima City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | Latitude = 33|Latitude_min = 32|Latitude_sec = 13.18 | Longitude =130|Longitude_min = 14|Longitude_sec = 33.04 | Map=Japan Fukuoka | deity=Iwanaga-hime no Mikoto
Konohanasakuya-hime no Mikoto | Shrine Rank=Village Shrine | Annual Festival=October 25 }} '''Sazareishi Shrine''' (細石神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Itoshima City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. Historically, it was written as "'''Sasareishi Shrine'''" (佐々禮石神社). The enshrined deities are the sister goddesses [[Iwanaga-hime]] and [[Konohanasakuya-hime]]. Under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|former shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a village shrine. == Overview == The shrine is believed to be located in the central area of the ancient [[Itokoku]] region. According to the "Sazareishi Shrine Origin Record" from the 8th year of [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] (1695), the shrine once had many sacred rice fields and was a large shrine. However, it suffered from repeated military conflicts, leading to the destruction of its shrine buildings. Furthermore, during the [[Taikō land survey]] conducted by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the 15th year of [[:en:Tenshō|Tenshō]] (1587), the shrine's sacred rice fields were confiscated, causing its decline. Currently, a stone pillar inscribed with "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Shrine" stands beside the torii gate. == Access == Approximately 10 minutes by bus towards Iharayama from [[:en:Hatae Station|Hatae Station]] on the [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] operated by [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]]. Get off at Mikumo Shrine (Sazareishi Shrine) bus stop. == References == * ''Itoshima and Ido Geography Complete Encyclopedia'' by Shoyu Yui * ''Shrines of Guardian Deities'' by Itoshima Shinto Priest Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} == References == * "Itoshima Geographical Encyclopedia" by Shusuke Yui. * "Shrines of Itoshima" by the Itoshima Shinto Priest Association. {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:細石神社]] == References == * ''Itoshima Shima Chiri Zenshi'' by Yui Shōyū * ''Shrine of the Guardian Deity'' by the Itoshima Shinto Clergy Association {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|細石神社|qq=Sazareishi Shrine|version=74993435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:細石神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] {{Commons category|Sazareishi-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11606084}}

Historically referred to as "Sasareiishi Jinja" (佐々禮石神社), the enshrined deities are the sister goddesses {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|磐長姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワナガヒメ}} and {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}. According to the shrine history titled "Sazareishi Jinja Goengi," compiled in the [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] era (1695), the shrine was once a large and significant establishment with extensive sacred rice fields. However, repeated conflicts resulted in the destruction of the shrine's buildings. Furthermore, during the {{ill|Taikō land survey|ja|太閤検地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taikō land survey|1=Taikō kenchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193888}} conducted by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] in the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] era (1587), the shrine's sacred lands were confiscated, leading to its decline. Today, a stone pillar labeled "Village Shrine, Sazareishi Jinja" stands next to the torii gate. == Access == The shrine can be reached by taking a bus bound for Iharayama from [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|JR Kyushu]]'s [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]] at [[:en:Hatae Station|Hatae Station]].
